mock_tests.instruction:
The Question Paper contains three sections.
 Section A has 24 questions. Attempt any 20 questions.
 Section B has 24 questions. Attempt any 20 questions.
 Section C has 12 questions. Attempt any 10 questions.
 All questions carry equal marks.
 There is no negative marking.

questions.question: The existence of a wide range of different types of organisms in a given place at a given time is_______.
questions.question_raw: The existence of a wide range of different types of organisms in a given place at a given time is_______.
questions.explanation: The parks and zoo are the places where wild animals are kept in protected environments under human care. Museums have collections of preserved plant and animal specimens for study and reference. Hence, the correct option is Biodiversity.

questions.question: What is a tonoplast?
questions.question_raw: What is a tonoplast?

options.text: Membrane boundary of the vacuole of plant cells.
Cell membrane of a plant cell.

correct_option_texts: Membrane boundary of the vacuole of plant cells.
questions.explanation: The membrane boundary of the vacuole of plant cells is tonoplast which is a selectively permeable membrane.

questions.question: An algae having ribbon-shaped chloroplast is_______.
questions.question_raw: An algae having ribbon-shaped chloroplast is_______.
questions.explanation: The shape of a chloroplast varies from species to species. It may be cup-shaped (e.g., Chlamydomonas), (e.g., Vaucheria), Girdle (e.g., Ulothrix), Stellate or Star-shaped (e.g., Zygnema), Reticulate or net-like (e.g., Cladophora, Oedogonium), Spiral or ribbon or scalariform (e.g., Spirogyra), ovoid or disc or spheroid in higher plants.

questions.question: Which of the following pairs of animals has non-glandular skin?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following pairs of animals has non-glandular skin?
questions.explanation: Chameleon and Turtle both are reptiles, having non-glandular skin.

questions.question: The parietal placentation is seen in:
questions.question_raw: The parietal placentation is seen in:
questions.explanation: In parietal placentation, the ovules develop on the inner wall of the ovary or on the peripheral part. The ovary is one-chambered but it becomes two-chambered due to the formation of the false septum, e.g., mustard and Argemone.

questions.question: Choose the odd one
questions.question_raw: Choose the odd one
questions.explanation: Cartilage

questions.question: Which one is not a part of the Golgi complex?
questions.question_raw: Which one is not a part of the Golgi complex?
questions.explanation: Golgi complex or Golgi body consists of cisternae, tubules and spherical vesicles. Glycosome is not present in the Golgi complex.

questions.question: Proteins perform many physiological functions. For example, some function as enzymes. Which of the following represents an additional function that some proteins discharge?
questions.question_raw: Proteins perform many physiological functions. For example, some function as enzymes. Which of the following represents an additional function that some proteins discharge?
questions.explanation: Some hormones that are products of endocrine glands are proteins or peptides, others are steroids.

questions.question: The joined area between adjacent cells are ________.
questions.question_raw: The joined area between adjacent cells are ________.
questions.explanation: A desmosome is a cell structure specialised for the cell to cell adhesion. They are located on lateral sides of plasma membranes.

questions.question: One full turn of DNA strands involves:
questions.question_raw: One full turn of DNA strands involves:
questions.explanation: One full turn of the helical strand would involve ten steps or ten base pairs. The pitch would be 34$\mathop {\text{A}}\limits^{\text{o}} $.

questions.question:
Which is the incorrect statement from the following?
 Lion, tiger, leopard, and cat belong to the same genus.
 A family is a group of related genera with still less number of similarities as compared to genus and species.
 Taxonomic aids are the prime source of taxonomy, systematics, and future studies.
 Systematics takes into account the evolutionary relationship between organisms.
questions.question_raw:
Which is the incorrect statement from the following?

  1. Lion, tiger, leopard, and cat belong to the same genus.
  2. A family is a group of related genera with still less number of similarities as compared to genus and species.
  3. Taxonomic aids are the prime source of taxonomy, systematics, and future studies.
  4. Systematics takes into account the evolutionary relationship between organisms.
questions.explanation: Lion (Panthera leo), leopard (P. pardus), and tiger (P. Tigris) with several common features are all species of the genus Panthera. The genus Felisincludes cats. Hence, the correct option is Lion, tiger, leopard and cat belong to the same genus.

questions.question: Double helix model of DNA was proposed by proposed by:
questions.question_raw: Double helix model of DNA was proposed by proposed by:
questions.explanation: Double helix model of DNA was proposed by James Watson and Francis Crick in 1953 based on X-ray diffraction data produced by Maurice Wilkins and Rosalind Franklin.

questions.question: Fusion of two motile gametes which are dissimilar in size is termed as:
questions.question_raw: Fusion of two motile gametes which are dissimilar in size is termed as:
questions.explanation: Anisogamy is the fusion of two dissimilar mobile gametes.

questions.question: Greatest extent of adaptation to various environments is found in_______.
questions.question_raw: Greatest extent of adaptation to various environments is found in_______.
questions.explanation: Adaptation is essential for the survival of the living organism. The greatest extent of adaption in various environmental conditions is found in Angiosperms. Angiosperms are found in all kinds of environments and altitudes.

questions.question: What is the name of modified root in turnip?
questions.question_raw: What is the name of modified root in turnip?
questions.explanation: The taproot in turnip is modified for food storage.

questions.question: Mark the odd one in the following:
questions.question_raw: Mark the odd one in the following:
questions.explanation: Protonema

questions.question: A clear zone around Golgi apparatus is zone of _______.
questions.question_raw: A clear zone around Golgi apparatus is zone of _______.
questions.explanation: The clear zone surrounding the Golgi apparatus is a zone of exclusion, where no other cellular activity takes place.

questions.question: Select the group of fat-soluble vitamins from the followings:
questions.question_raw: Select the group of fat-soluble vitamins from the followings:

options.text: Vitamins A, B, C and D
Vitamins B, C, D and K
Vitamins A, D, E, and K

correct_option_texts: Vitamins A, D, E, and K
questions.explanation: Vitamins A, D, E, and K are fat-soluble but insoluble in water. Vitamin B and C are water-soluble vitamins.

questions.question: Animals and plants are best protected in:
questions.question_raw: Animals and plants are best protected in:
questions.explanation: National parks provide the best protection with the animals and plants living in a particular habitat. As plants and animals live and survive in a natural habitat without the interference of humans.

questions.question: The cell lining the blood vessels are:
questions.question_raw: The cell lining the blood vessels are:
questions.explanation: The endothelium is a type of epithelium that lines the interior surface of blood vessels and lymphatic vessels, forming an interface between circulating blood or lymph in the lumen and the rest of the vessel wall. It is a thin layer of simple squamous called endothelial cells.

questions.question: The world’s highly prized wool yielding Paschimina breed is:
questions.question_raw: The world’s highly prized wool yielding Paschimina breed is:
questions.explanation: Wool is generally obtained from sheep but the world’s highly prized wool yielding Paschimina breed is a goat generally reared in Kashmir valley. The wool is very soft with a lot of warmth.

questions.question: When the stamens in a flower remain free, it is said to be______.
questions.question_raw: When the stamens in a flower remain free, it is said to be______.
questions.explanation: The stamens in a flower may either remain free (polyandrous) or maybe united in varying degrees.

questions.question: What is the name of structure which makes the compound eyes of a cockroach?
questions.question_raw: What is the name of structure which makes the compound eyes of a cockroach?
questions.explanation: Compound eyes of arthropods like a cockroach is made of tiny units called ommatidia. Each Ommatidium helps to form an image.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Arachidonic acid is an unsaturated fatty acid.
Reason (R): There are present one or more double bonds between carbon atoms in unsaturated fatty acid.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): Arachidonic acid is an unsaturated fatty acid.
Reason (R): There are present one or more double bonds between carbon atoms in unsaturated fatty acid.

options.text: A is false but R is true.
A is true but R is false.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.

correct_option_texts: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
questions.explanation: Arachidonic acid is an unsaturated fatty acid due to the presence of double bond. Arachidonic acid is a carboxylic acid with a 20-carbon chain and four cis-double bonds; the first double bond is located at the sixth carbon from the omega end.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Homology is due to anatomical similarity and functional dissimilarity.
Reason (R): Fins of fishes and the flippers of whales are homologous organs.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): Homology is due to anatomical similarity and functional dissimilarity.
Reason (R): Fins of fishes and the flippers of whales are homologous organs.

options.text: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
A is true but R is false.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is false but R is true.

correct_option_texts: A is true but R is false.
questions.explanation: Homologous organs are those organs which have the same essential structure, but they are differently modified in adaptation to different functions. Analogous organs are those which have similar structure connected with similarity of function, have superficial resemblance to one another, but are of unlike origin. For example fins of fishes and the flippers of whales serve the same function and hence are analogous.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): The number of cells in a multicellular organism is inversely proportional to size of body.
Reason (R): All cells of the biological world are alive.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): The number of cells in a multicellular organism is inversely proportional to size of body.
Reason (R): All cells of the biological world are alive.

options.text: A is false but R is true.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is true but R is false.
Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.

correct_option_texts: A is false but R is true.
questions.explanation: The size and shape of the cell in multicellular organism depends upon the location and function performed by them.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Pinus embryo has Two cotyledons.
Reason (R): Pinus shows polyembryony.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): Pinus embryo has Two cotyledons.
Reason (R): Pinus shows polyembryony.

options.text: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
A is true but R is false.
A is false but R is true.

correct_option_texts: A is false but R is true.
questions.explanation: In Pinus, the embryo is straight and consists of a short axis bearing a ring of about ten slender, yellow cotyledons at the end, away from the micropylar end. In Pinus, more than one embryo comes from a single egg by the splitting of the product of a single fertilization, termed as cleavage polyembryony.

questions.question: Which is the second largest Phylum in the Kingdom Animals?
questions.question_raw: Which is the second largest Phylum in the Kingdom Animals?
questions.explanation: Mollusca is the second largest phyllum after Arthrhopoda.

questions.question: Pentamerous, actinomorphic flower, bicarpellary ovary with oblique septum and berry or capsule fruit belong to the family______.
questions.question_raw: Pentamerous, actinomorphic flower, bicarpellary ovary with oblique septum and berry or capsule fruit belong to the family______.
questions.explanation: The members of family Solanaceae bears flower which is pentamerous, actinomorphic, bicarpellary, ovary with oblique septum and berry, or capsule fruit. It includes potato, brinjal, etc.

questions.question: Find out the odd one in the following:
questions.question_raw: Find out the odd one in the following:
questions.explanation: Antennae

questions.question: Which one is the cofactor for the proteolytic enzyme carboxypeptidase?
questions.question_raw: Which one is the cofactor for the proteolytic enzyme carboxypeptidase?
questions.explanation: Zinc is a cofactor for the pro­teolytic enzyme carboxypeptidase and forms coordina­tion bonds with the side chains of two histidines and one glutamic acid residue at the active site. A fourth bond is formed between zinc and the a-carboxyl group of the substrate amino acids, and it is here that the cleavage of the peptide occurs.

questions.question: The taxonomical aid used for identification of plants and animals based on the similarities and dissimilarities is _______.
questions.question_raw: The taxonomical aid used for identification of plants and animals based on the similarities and dissimilarities is _______.
questions.explanation: Zoo and parks are the places where wild animals are kept in protected environments under human care. Museums have collections of preserved plant and animal specimens for study and reference. Hence, the correct option is Key.

questions.question: Which one is not a difference between Prokaryotic and Eukaryotic cells?
questions.question_raw: Which one is not a difference between Prokaryotic and Eukaryotic cells?
questions.explanation: Prokaryotic and Eukaryotic cells and differentiated on the basis of the number of chromosomes, presence or absence of nuclear membrane and presence and absence of membrane-bounded organelles. The cell wall is not a difference.

questions.question: Which of the following functions is carried out by Moss peristome?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following functions is carried out by Moss peristome?
questions.explanation: All moss capsules have an operculum and peristome. During spore dispersal, peristome teeth bend outwards and spores fall out of the capsule and are carried by air currents.

questions.question: Water vascular system is the characteristic of which group of the following:
questions.question_raw: Water vascular system is the characteristic of which group of the following:
questions.explanation: Echinodermata contain water vascular system that help in nuttrition and respiration.

questions.question: Bladders of Utricularia and Pitchers of Nepenthes are a modification of______.
questions.question_raw: Bladders of Utricularia and Pitchers of Nepenthes are a modification of______.
questions.explanation: Bladders of Utricularia and Pitcher of Nepenthes are a modification of leaves. They are modified to catch insects that are insectivorous plants.

questions.question: Which of the following leucocytes transforms into macrophages?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following leucocytes transforms into macrophages?
questions.explanation: Monocytes are white blood cells or leucocytes that transform into macrophages to engulf the pathogen entering the body.

questions.question: Select the incorrect matching.
questions.question_raw: Select the incorrect matching.
questions.explanation: The nucleus contains the chromosomes which in turn contain the genetic material, DNA.

questions.question: What is the name of heterocyclic carbon compound attached to sugar?
questions.question_raw: What is the name of heterocyclic carbon compound attached to sugar?
questions.explanation: A nucleoside is formed by the linkage of the nitrogenous base to the -OH group at the first carbon of the pentose sugar through an N-glycosyl linkage.

questions.question: The highest categories of classification is ________.
questions.question_raw: The highest categories of classification is ________.
questions.explanation:
The taxonomic hierarchy Kingdom $\rightarrow$ Phylum/Division $\rightarrow$ Class $\rightarrow$ Order $\rightarrow$ Family $\rightarrow$ Genus $\rightarrow$ Species.
Hence, the correct option is the Kingdom.

questions.question: What is the function of adventitious roots?
questions.question_raw: What is the function of adventitious roots?
questions.explanation: Some roots, called adventitious roots, arise from an organ other than the root - usually a stem, sometimes a leaf. They are especially numerous on underground stems. The formation of adventitious roots makes it possible to vegetatively propagate many plants from stem or leaf cuttings.

questions.question: Which one of the following is the correct pairing of a body part and muscle fibre that moves it?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following is the correct pairing of a body part and muscle fibre that moves it?

options.text: Biceps of upper arm - smooth muscle fibres

questions.explanation: The iris contains involuntary smooth muscles. It encircles the pupil of the iris and helps to dilate the pupil when there is insufficient light for the normal function of the eye.

questions.question: Genus represents
questions.question_raw: Genus represents

options.text: A collection of plants or animals
Group of closely related species of plants or animals
Group of Differentially elated species of plants or animals

correct_option_texts: Group of closely related species of plants or animals
questions.explanation: Taxon genus is just above the taxon species

questions.question: The structural element of chromatin is _________.
questions.question_raw: The structural element of chromatin is _________.
questions.explanation: Chromatin is the thread-like structure present in the nucleus. Chromatin has beads like structure called nucleosome that contain histone protein and DNA.

questions.question: In which pteridophytes, heterosporous is produced?
questions.question_raw: In which pteridophytes, heterosporous is produced?
questions.explanation: Genera like Selaginella and Salvinia which produce two kinds of spores, macro (large) and micro (small) spores are known as heterosporous.

questions.question: Which phylum has comb plates for locomotion as their distinctive feature?
questions.question_raw: Which phylum has comb plates for locomotion as their distinctive feature?
questions.explanation: The body of phylum Ctenophora bears eight external rows of ciliated comb plates, which help in locomotion.

questions.question: Fibroblasts, macrophages, and mast cells are present in ______.
questions.question_raw: Fibroblasts, macrophages, and mast cells are present in ______.
questions.explanation: Apart from being connective tissue, Areolar tissue is also protective in nature. It has a large number of fibroblast cells that produce fibres. Mast cells, plasma cells, and macrophages help to protect against pathogens and aid in tissue repair.

questions.question:
Instructions for Question 49 to 54:
The morphology of the mycelium, mode of spore formation and fruiting bodies form the basis for the division of the fungi kingdom into various classes which include four sub-division Phycomycetes, ascomycetes, basidiomycetes, deuteromycetes. Members of phycomycetes are found in aquatic habitats and on decaying wood in moist and damp places or as obligate parasites on plants, ascomycetes are mostly multicellular. The asexual spores are conidia produced exogenously on the special mycelium called conidiophores. Basidiomycetes are mushrooms, bracket fungi or puffballs. They grow in soil, on logs and tree stumps and in living plant bodies as parasites. The basidiospores are exogenously produced on the basidium.
The asexual spores are not found, vegetative reproduction occurs by fragmentation and sexual organs are absent. Identify the class of fungi.

questions.question: Many pulses of daily use belong to one of the families below (tick the correct answer)
questions.question_raw: Many pulses of daily use belong to one of the families below (tick the correct answer)
questions.explanation: Fabaceae are the sources of pulses such as gram, arhar, sem, moong, soybean.

questions.question: The haemoglobin content per 100 ml of blood of a normal healthy human adult is:
questions.question_raw: The haemoglobin content per 100 ml of blood of a normal healthy human adult is:
questions.explanation: The haemoglobin content per 100 ml of blood of a normal healthy human adult is in the range of 12-16 g. Haemoglobin acts as a respiratory carrier. It is responsible for picking up oxygen from the lungs and transporting it to all parts of the body through blood. Maintaining a healthy level of haemoglobin in blood is essential for sufficient supply of oxygen to cells or tissues. Level of haemoglobin less than 12g/100ml leads to anaemia and weakness.

questions.question: Protein synthesis takes place in which cell organelle?
questions.question_raw: Protein synthesis takes place in which cell organelle?
questions.explanation: Protein synthesis occurs in cellular structures called ribosomes, found outside the nucleus. The process by which genetic information is transferred from the nucleus to the ribosomes is called transcription.

questions.question: The most abundant organic molecule present on earth is______.
questions.question_raw: The most abundant organic molecule present on earth is______.
questions.explanation: Cellulose is the most abundant organic molecule present on earth. Cellulose forms the cell wall of all plant cells. The cell wall is the outermost protective layer of the cell.

questions.question: Which of the following plastids stores starch?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following plastids stores starch?
questions.explanation: Amyloplasts are non-pigmented organelles found in some plant cells. They are responsible for the synthesis and storage of starch granules, through the polymerization of glucose. Amyloplasts also convert this starch back into sugar when the plant needs energy.

questions.question: The most abundant component of living organisms is:
questions.question_raw: The most abundant component of living organisms is:
questions.explanation: Water is the most abundant component of living organism, made up of hydrogen and oxygen.

questions.question: Which serve as quick referral systems in taxonomical studies?
questions.question_raw: Which serve as quick referral systems in taxonomical studies?
questions.explanation: Museums have collections of preserved plant and animal specimens for study and reference. Manuals are useful in providing information for the identification of names of species found in an area. Monographs contain information on anyone's taxon. Hence, the correct option is Herbarium.

questions.question: Cytoskeleton is made of.
questions.question_raw: Cytoskeleton is made of.
questions.explanation: Cytoskeleton is a series of intercellular proteins that help a cell with shape, support andmovement. So, cytoskeleton is made up of proteinaceous filaments.

questions.question_raw: What should be TDS of Ca++ and Mg++ in soft water?
questions.explanation: TDS stands for total dissolved solids which are inorganic salts dissolved in water. The TDS of soft water should be up to 120 ppm.

questions.question: Which of the following organisms have a prokaryotic cell?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following organisms have a prokaryotic cell?
questions.explanation: Cyanobacteria, also known as Cyanophyta, is a phylum of bacteria that obtain their energy through photosynthesis, and are the only photosynthetic prokaryotes able to produce oxygen.

questions.question: In most fungi, the structural polysaccharide of the cell wall is ________.
questions.question_raw: In most fungi, the structural polysaccharide of the cell wall is ________.
questions.explanation: In most fungi the structural polysaccharide of the cell wall is chitin. In the plant cells, the cell wall is made up of cellulose.

questions.question: Which genus has a large number of closely related species?
questions.question_raw: Which genus has a large number of closely related species?
questions.explanation: A genus consists of a large number of closed related species. Genus Solanum consist of larger common species that include potato, tomato, brinjal, etc.

questions.question: In yeast, during fermentation, the glycolysis pathway leads to:
questions.question_raw: In yeast, during fermentation, the glycolysis pathway leads to:
questions.explanation: Yeast cells obtain energy under anaerobic conditions using a very similar process called alcoholic fermentation. Glycolysis is the chemical breakdown of glucose to lactic acid. Alcoholic fermentation is identical to glycolysis except for the final step. In alcoholic fermentation, pyruvic acid is broken down into ethanol and carbon dioxide.

questions.question: The yellow or brown spots which have sporangia in fern are called ______.
questions.question_raw: The yellow or brown spots which have sporangia in fern are called ______.
questions.explanation: The yellow or brown spots which have sporangia in fern are called sori. Spores are produced inside these sporangia to produce haploid gametophyte.

questions.question: Which one of the following is a vascular cryptogam?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following is a vascular cryptogam?
questions.explanation: A Cryptogam is a plant that reproduces by spores without flowers or seeds. Most of the cryptogamous plants lack vascular bundles i.e xylem and phloem but some member of pteridophytes like Equisetum contain vascular bundle.

questions.question: In Ocimum sanctum (tulsi), stem is_____.
questions.question_raw: In Ocimum sanctum (tulsi), stem is_____.
questions.explanation: The stem of Ocimum sanctum or tulsi is quadrangular in shape. The stem is covered with hairy projection.

questions.question: Mature erythrocytes cannot utilise glucose because they lack_______.
questions.question_raw: Mature erythrocytes cannot utilise glucose because they lack_______.
questions.explanation: Mature erythrocytes or red blood corpuscles cannot utilize glucose because these cells lack mitochondria as well as the nucleus. Though glycolysis or glucose breakdown occurs in the cytoplasm, the final breakdown of pyruvate takes place inside mitochondria. Also, the electron transport chain is located inside mitochondria. Hence, the presence of mitochondria is essential for a cell to utilize glucose and release energy.

questions.question: Each centriole is surrounded by dense amorphous protoplasmic masses called:
questions.question_raw: Each centriole is surrounded by dense amorphous protoplasmic masses called:
questions.explanation: All animal cell has two small organelles called centrioles. Each centriole is surrounded by dense amorphous protoplasmic masses called Massules.

questions.explanation: On average, females produce 9-10 oothecae. Each ootheca contains 14-16 eggs.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Enzymes lower the activation energy.
Reason (R): A substrate molecule can be acted upon by a particular enzyme.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): Enzymes lower the activation energy.
Reason (R): A substrate molecule can be acted upon by a particular enzyme.

options.text: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
A is true but R is false.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is false but R is true.

correct_option_texts: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
questions.explanation: Activation energy is an external supply of energy that is needed for the initiation of the chemical reaction. The activation energy required for such a large number of reactions cannot be provided by living systems. Enzymes lower the activation energy required for a reaction. Enzymes are generally specific for their substrates.

Reason (R): Cave animals lack eyes.

Reason (R): Algae also possess the archegonium.

questions.question_raw: The number of nymphs produced from single ootheca of the frog is _______.
questions.explanation: Young ones of frogs are called nymphs. When the ootheca ruptures, 16 nymphs come out.

questions.question: What is the difference between the taxonomic categories of division and phylum?
questions.question_raw: What is the difference between the taxonomic categories of division and phylum?

The phylum is a more inclusive category

questions.question:
Instructions for Question 49 to 54:
The fungi constitute a unique kingdom of heterotrophic organisms. They show a great diversity in morphology and habitat. Fungi are cosmopolitan and occur in air, water, soil, and on animals and plants. They prefer to grow in warm and humid places. Most fungi are heterotrophic and absorb soluble organic matter from dead substrates and hence are called saprophytes. When a fungus reproduces sexually, two haploid hyphae of compatible mating types come together and fuse. In some fungi, the fusion of two haploid cells immediately results in diploid cells (2n). The fungiform fruiting bodies in which reduction division occurs, leading to the formation of haploid spores. Symbionts – in association with algae as lichens and with roots of higher plants as mycorrhiza.
The sexual cycle of fungi involves which of the following steps?

questions.question_raw: Ions connected with forming cross-bridge is______.
questions.explanation: Ions connected with forming cross-bridge is calcium ions (Ca^2+) that help the impulse to cross the gaps at the synapse.

questions.question: Which of the following elements is highest in percentage composition in the human body?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following elements is highest in percentage composition in the human body?
questions.explanation: By mass, oxygen is the most abundant element in the human body. most of the body consists of water or H_2O. Oxygen accounts for 61-65% of the mass of the human body. Even though there are many more atoms of hydrogen in your body than oxygen, each oxygen atom is 16 times more massive than a hydrogen atom.

mock_tests.instruction:
The Question Paper contains three sections.
 Section A has 24 questions. Attempt any 20 questions.
 Section B has 24 questions. Attempt any 20 questions.
 Section C has 12 questions. Attempt any 10 questions.
 All questions carry equal marks.
 There is no negative marking.

questions.question: International Code of Biological Nomenclature is applied to_______.
questions.question_raw: International Code of Biological Nomenclature is applied to_______.
questions.explanation: Since biology deals with both plants and animals, the international code of biological nomenclature is applied to both plants and animals. Hence, the correct option is Both plant and animals.

questions.question: Which does not occur in cell membrane?
questions.question_raw: Which does not occur in cell membrane?
questions.explanation: The cell membrane is made up of lipid bilayer consisting of different components like phospholipids, glycolipids, and cholesterol. However, proline is an alpha-amino acid used in protein synthesis and is a major constituent of collagen.

questions.question: Angiosperm: roots :: Rhodophyta :?
questions.question_raw: Angiosperm: roots :: Rhodophyta :?
questions.explanation: Angiosperms plants have roots to hold the soil and remain erect. Similarly, Rhodophytes have holdfast by which they remain attached with substratum to absorb water and minerals.

questions.question: What is the name of the excretory organ in Arthropoda?
questions.question_raw: What is the name of the excretory organ in Arthropoda?
questions.explanation: Malpighian tubules is the excretory organ of Arthropoda. The system consists of branching tubules extending from the alimentary canal that absorbs solutes, water, and wastes from the surrounding hemolymph.

questions.question: A plant in which adventitious roots develop chlorophyll and perform photosynthesis is______.
questions.question_raw: A plant in which adventitious roots develop chlorophyll and perform photosynthesis is______.
questions.explanation: Dischidia

questions.question: Find out the odd one in the following
questions.question_raw: Find out the odd one in the following
questions.explanation: Ligament

questions.question: The most recent and accepted model of the plasma membrane is:
questions.question_raw: The most recent and accepted model of the plasma membrane is:
questions.explanation: Fluid-Mosaic model is the most recent and accepted model of the plasma membrane. This model was proposed by Singer and Nicolson in 1972. The quasi-fluid nature of lipid enables the lateral movement of proteins within the overall bilayer.

questions.question: Which of the following nitrogenous base produces nucleoside only with ribose sugar?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following nitrogenous base produces nucleoside only with ribose sugar?
questions.explanation: Uracil nitrogenous base is produced nucleoside only with ribose sugar. This nucleoside is present only inside the RNA and absent in DNA.

questions.question: Who proposed the fluid mosaic model of plasma membrane?
questions.question_raw: Who proposed the fluid mosaic model of plasma membrane?
questions.explanation: Singer and Nicolson in 1972. It is the most widely accepted model. According to this, biological membranes are quasi fluid structures in which lipids and proteins are arranged in a mosaic manner.

questions.question: Number of fatty acid residues found in one molecule of fat is_____.
questions.question_raw: Number of fatty acid residues found in one molecule of fat is_____.
questions.explanation: Fat consists of molecules of fatty acids and molecules of glycerine. Each molecule of fat contains three molecules of fatty acids and one molecule of glycerine.

questions.question: Botanical gardens and zoological parks have
questions.question_raw: Botanical gardens and zoological parks have

options.text: Collection of exotic living species only
Collection of endemic living species only
Collection of endemic and exotic living species
Collection of only local plants and animals

correct_option_texts: Collection of endemic and exotic living species
questions.explanation: To understand this, you can visit the zoo at Delhi or Kolkata. You can also search in Google image and you will find giraffes; along with tigers and lions. Tigers and lions are endemic species while giraffe is an exotic species.

questions.question: Enzymes catalyzing in the link together of two compounds are ________.
questions.question_raw: Enzymes catalyzing in the link together of two compounds are ________.
questions.explanation: Ligases catalyze the linking together of 2 compounds, e.g., enzymes that catalyze joining of C-O, C-S, C-N, P-O, etc. bonds.

questions.question: Which one is living fossil _______.
questions.question_raw: Which one is living fossil _______.
questions.explanation: Ginkgo is a living fossil. This plant belongs to the gymnosperms division. It is considered as one of the oldest plants known and still surviving in some parts of the world.

questions.question: The phenomenon of having two types of spores by the same plant is called ________.
questions.question_raw: The phenomenon of having two types of spores by the same plant is called ________.
questions.explanation: The phenomenon of having two types of spores by the same plant is called heterospory. Male spores are small (micro) and female spores are comparatively larger (macro) than males like Selaginella and Salvinia are heterosporous.

questions.question: The cup-like structure formed by the adnation of sepals, petals, and stamens is ______.
questions.question_raw: The cup-like structure formed by the adnation of sepals, petals, and stamens is ______.
questions.explanation: In angiosperms, a hypanthium is a structure where basal portions of the calyx, the corolla, and the stamens form a cup-shaped tube.

questions.question: Where do we find aerolar tissue?
questions.question_raw: Where do we find aerolar tissue?
questions.explanation: Areolar tissue is a typical connective tissue. One of the locations of this tissue in our body is below the skin.

questions.question: Which of the following is correct:
questions.question_raw: Which of the following is correct:

options.text: Cells are formed de novo from abiotic materials
Both animal and plant cells have a well defined cell wall
In prokaryotes, there are no membrane bound organelles
Cells of all living organisms have a nucleus

correct_option_texts: In prokaryotes, there are no membrane bound organelles
questions.explanation: In prokaryotes, there are no membrane bound organelles

questions.question: The formation of both peptide and glycosidic bonds involves:
questions.question_raw: The formation of both peptide and glycosidic bonds involves:
questions.explanation: In a polypeptide or a protein, amino acids are linked by a peptide bond which is formed when the carboxyl (-COOH) group of one amino acid reacts with the amino (-NH_2) group of the next amino acid with the elimination of a water moiety (the process is called dehydration). In polysaccharide, the individual monosaccharides are linked by a glycosidic bond. This bond is also formed by dehydration.

questions.question: Which of the following are characteristics of living things
questions.question_raw: Which of the following are characteristics of living things
questions.explanation: Life is a unique, complex organisation of molecules, expressing itself through chemical reactions which lead to growth, development, responsiveness, adaptation and reproduction.

questions.question: Study of tissues is called:
questions.question_raw: Study of tissues is called:
questions.explanation: Study of organisms at tissues level is called histology. It includes the study of tissue structure, origin, location and function.

questions.question: A plant that forms fruits but not seeds are ______.
questions.question_raw: A plant that forms fruits but not seeds are ______.
questions.explanation: Generally, seeds are formed inside the fruits. But in banana viable seeds are not present. It is a parthenocarpic fruit.

questions.question: Book lungs are respiratory organ of______.
questions.question_raw: Book lungs are respiratory organ of______.
questions.explanation: Book lung is a type of respiratory organ used for atmospheric gas exchange that is found in Arachnida such as scorpions and spiders.

questions.question: Which is not a function of leaves?
questions.question_raw: Which is not a function of leaves?
questions.explanation: Leaves perform the function of photosynthesis, exchange of gases, and transpiration to lose excess of water. The formation of new buds is not a function of leaves.

questions.question: The type of muscles present in our:
questions.question_raw: The type of muscles present in our:

options.text: the intestine is striated and involuntary.
the upper arm is smooth muscle and fusiform in shape.
the heart is involuntary and unstriated smooth muscle.
the thigh is striated and voluntary.

correct_option_texts: the thigh is striated and voluntary.
questions.explanation: The muscle present in our thigh is striated and voluntary muscles. They are attached to the bone for movement and works according to our will.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): The higher the turn-over number the more efficient an enzyme is.
Reason (R): It is not dependent upon the number of active sites present over an enzyme.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): The higher the turn-over number the more efficient an enzyme is.
Reason (R): It is not dependent upon the number of active sites present over an enzyme.

options.text: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is true but R is false.
Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
A is false but R is true.

correct_option_texts: A is true but R is false.
questions.explanation: The number of substrate molecules changed per minute by a molecule or enzyme is called the turnover number. The higher the turn-over number, the more efficient an enzyme is. It depends upon the number of active sites present over an enzyme.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Study of internal structure is called anatomy.
Reason (R): It is useful for phylogenetic study.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): Study of internal structure is called anatomy.
Reason (R): It is useful for phylogenetic study.

options.text: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is true but R is false.
A is false but R is true.
Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.

correct_option_texts: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
questions.explanation: Anatomy is the study of internal structure that can be observed with an unaided eye after dissection. By studying the anatomy of a large number of organisms, it is useful for knowing phylogenetic similarity (homology) and phylogenetic dissimilarity (analogy).

questions.question:
Assertion (A): The lipids are arranged within the membrane with the non-polar head towards the outer sides and the polar tails towards the inner part.
Reason (R): This ensures that the nonpolar tail of saturated hydrocarbons is protected from the aqueous environment.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): The lipids are arranged within the membrane with the non-polar head towards the outer sides and the polar tails towards the inner part.
Reason (R): This ensures that the nonpolar tail of saturated hydrocarbons is protected from the aqueous environment.

options.text: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
A is false but R is true.
A is true but R is false.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.

correct_option_texts: A is false but R is true.
questions.explanation: The cell membrane is composed of lipids that are arranged in a bilayer. Also, the lipids are arranged within the membrane with the polar head towards the outer sides and the hydrophobic tails towards the inner part. This ensures that the nonpolar tail of saturated hydrocarbons is protected from the aqueous environment.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Batrachospermum shows alternation of generations.
Reason (R): Haploid zygote stage is alternate with diploid stage.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): Batrachospermum shows alternation of generations.
Reason (R): Haploid zygote stage is alternate with diploid stage.

options.text: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is false but R is true.
A is true but R is false.
Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.

correct_option_texts: A is true but R is false.
questions.explanation:
Batrachospermum plant is a free-living gametophyte. It produces male and female gametes which fuse to form the zygote. The zygote is the only diploid structure in the lifecycle. There are three haploid somatic phases in the life of Batrchospermum, which are
 First is the gametophyte Batrachospermum plant.
 The second is the haploid, dependent carposporophyte (parasitic on the parent gametophyte).
 The third haploid phase is the chantransia stage. It is free-living and is formed by the germination of the haploid carpospore.

questions.question: The voice producing organ of birds is called as:
questions.question_raw: The voice producing organ of birds is called as:
questions.explanation: The voice producing organ of birds is called Syrinx. Voice is produced due to pressure variation. In human beings voice producing organ is called the larynx.

questions.question: Which part of the plant help to hold the blade to light?
questions.question_raw: Which part of the plant help to hold the blade to light?
questions.explanation: The petiole help hold the blade to light. Long thin flexible petioles allow leaf blades to flutter in wind, thereby cooling the leaf and bringing fresh air to the leaf surface.

questions.question: Which of the following organs will not have ciliated epithelium?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following organs will not have ciliated epithelium?
questions.explanation: Small Intestine

questions.question: When we homogenize any tissue in acid the acid-soluble pool, it represents what?
questions.question_raw: When we homogenize any tissue in acid the acid-soluble pool, it represents what?
questions.explanation: The cytoplasm is left as an acid-soluble substance.

questions.question: A virus differ from a bacterium as it contains:
questions.question_raw: A virus differ from a bacterium as it contains:

options.text: DNA or RNA as genetic material with no ribosome

correct_option_texts: DNA or RNA as genetic material with no ribosome
questions.explanation: Virus differs from bacteria in terms of having DNA or RNA as genetic material with no ribosomes. The virus can replicate inside the host cell but become inactive outside the host cell.

questions.question: Elaioplasts belongs to:
questions.question_raw: Elaioplasts belongs to:
questions.explanation:
The leucoplasts are the colourless plastids of varied shapes and sizes with stored nutrients. Leucoplast includes:
 Amyloplasts storing carbohydrates.
 Elaioplasts storing oils and fats.
 Aleuroplasts storing proteins.

questions.question: Which among the following belong to Sphenopsida?
questions.question_raw: Which among the following belong to Sphenopsida?
questions.explanation: Equisetum of Pteridophyta belongs to class Sphenopsida.

questions.question: Haemocoel is found in:
questions.question_raw: Haemocoel is found in:
questions.explanation: Haemocoel is the main body cavity of invertebrates such as insects. It is also found in Pila which do not contain the blood circulating system.

questions.question: Which one of the following has the highest number of species in nature?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following has the highest number of species in nature?
questions.explanation: Insects have the highest number of species in nature. Insect belongs to arthropods family of class Mammalia. They are characterized by jointed legs.

questions.question: How many pairs of spiracles are found in cockroach?
questions.question_raw: How many pairs of spiracles are found in cockroach?
questions.explanation: The respiratory system consists of a network of the trachea. This network opens through 10 pairs of small holes called spiracles present on the lateral side of the body.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is true for a secretory cell?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following statements is true for a secretory cell?

options.text: Rough Endoplasmic Reticulum (RER) is easily observed in the cell.
Secretory granules are formed in nucleus.
Only Smooth Endoplasmic Reticulum (SER) is present.

correct_option_texts: Rough Endoplasmic Reticulum (RER) is easily observed in the cell.
questions.explanation: Rough Endoplasmic Reticulum (RER) is easily observed in the secretory cell.

questions.question: Which is the most abundant chemical for the living organisms?
questions.question_raw: Which is the most abundant chemical for the living organisms?
questions.explanation: Water is the most abundant chemical for living organisms. In adult men, about 60% of their bodies are water.

questions.question: National Botanical Institute is located in ________.
questions.question_raw: National Botanical Institute is located in ________.
questions.explanation: The national botanical research institute is located in Lucknow (UP). This is one of the largest botanical research institutes in the world housing thousands of plants.

questions.question: A flower which can be divided into two equal vertical halves by only one plane is called ________.
questions.question_raw: A flower which can be divided into two equal vertical halves by only one plane is called ________.
questions.explanation: The symmetry in which a flower can be divided into two equal vertical halves by only one plane is called zygomorphic flower as in pea.

questions.question: How many types of nephridia are seen in earthworm based on their location?
questions.question_raw: How many types of nephridia are seen in earthworm based on their location?
questions.explanation:
The excretory organs occur as segmentally arranged coiled tubules called nephridia.
They are of three types:
 septal nephridia
 integumentary nephridia
 pharyngeal nephridia.

questions.question: The scientific study of the diversity of organism and their evolutionary relationship is called:
questions.question_raw: The scientific study of the diversity of organism and their evolutionary relationship is called:
questions.explanation: Systematics is the scientific study of the diversity of organisms and their evolutionary relationship. Systematics is also known as taxonomy. It includes identification, nomenclature, and classification.

questions.question: The ability of a living somatic nucleated cell to form the complete organism is called:
questions.question_raw: The ability of a living somatic nucleated cell to form the complete organism is called:
questions.explanation: Cellular Totipotency is the ability of a single cell to produce all cell types and to organize them into an entire organism when cultured in a suitable culture medium at the appropriate temperature and aeration conditions. Spores and Zygote are examples of totipotent cells.

questions.question: Which of the following division contains the largest number of plant species?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following division contains the largest number of plant species?
questions.explanation: Angiosperms are found in almost every habitat from forests and grasslands to sea margins and deserts. There are an estimated 352,000 species of flowering plants.

questions.question: Silk produced by Antheraeamylitta is called ________.
questions.question_raw: Silk produced by Antheraeamylitta is called ________.
questions.explanation: The silk produced by Antheraeamylitta is called Tasar silk. This silk is considered as the most durable and best quality silk.

questions.question: The winter sleep in frog is called ________.
questions.question_raw: The winter sleep in frog is called ________.
questions.explanation: During extreme heat and cold periods, the frog takes shelter in deep burrows to protect them from extreme environmental conditions. This is called as summer sleep (aestivation) and winter sleep (hibernation).

questions.question:
Instructions for Question 49 to 54:
All single-celled eukaryotes are placed under Protista, but the boundaries of this kingdom are not well defined. Chrysophytes, Dinoflagellates, Euglenoids, Slime moulds and Protozoans under Protista. Members of Protista are primarily aquatic. This kingdom forms a link with the others dealing with plants, animals and fungi. Being eukaryotes, the protistan cell body contains a well-defined nucleus and other membrane-bound organelles. Some have flagella or cilia. Protists reproduce asexually and sexually by a process involving cell fusion and zygote formation. 
________ group of Protista include diatoms which are the chief producer in the ocean.

questions.question: In an inflorescence where flowers are borne laterally in an acropetal succession, the position of the youngest floral bud in the floral axis shall be

questions.question: Setae help in locomotion in earthworms but are not uniformly present in all the segments. They are present in:
questions.question_raw: Setae help in locomotion in earthworms but are not uniformly present in all the segments. They are present in:
questions.explanation: In each body segment of earthworm, except the first, last, and clitellum, there are rows of setae, embedded in the epidermal pits in the middle of each segment.

questions.question: The smallest free-living organism is______.
questions.question_raw: The smallest free-living organism is______.
questions.explanation: PPLO (Pleuro Pneumonia Like Organisms). They are generally smaller and multiply more rapidly than the eukaryotic cells.

questions.question_raw: Which of the following is not a macromolecule?
questions.explanation: Lipids are not technically macromolecules, because they’re not built in the same way though they are formed through dehydration reactions, they’re not polymers made up of individual subunits called monomers.

n = 18

questions.explanation: The pollen tubes enter the embryo-sac where two male gametes are discharged. One of the male gametes fuses with the egg cell to form a zygote (syngamy). questions.question: The number of amino acids found in proteins is _____.
questions.question_raw: The number of amino acids found in proteins is _____.
questions.explanation:
Proteins are made up of amino acids, which are organic compounds that combine to form the building blocks of proteins. There are 20 standard amino acids that are encoded by the universal genetic code and found in proteins. These include essential amino acids that must be obtained through the diet and non-essential amino acids that can be synthesized by the body.
Although there are some other amino acids like selenocysteine (sometimes referred to as the 21st amino acid) and pyrrolysine, these are not universally found in all proteins and are encoded by specific rare genetic mechanisms.

questions.question:
Instructions for Question 49 to 54:
R.H. Whittaker proposed a Five Kingdom Classification. The kingdoms defined by him were named Monera, Protista, Fungi, Plantae, and Animalia. The main criteria for classification used by him include cell structure, body organisation, mode of nutrition, reproduction and phylogenetic relationships. The three-domain system has also been proposed that divides the Kingdom Monera into two domains, leaving the remaining eukaryotic kingdoms in the third domain and thereby a six kingdom classification. Earlier classification systems included bacteria, blue-green algae, fungi, mosses, ferns, gymnosperms and the angiosperms under 'Plants'. The character that unified this whole kingdom was that all the organisms included had a cell wall in their cells.
Another Name of Cyanobacteria

questions.question_raw: Which one of the following is a true nut?
questions.explanation:
A true nut is a fruit composed of a hard shell and an edible seed. The shell does not open to release the seed (indehiscent), e.g., hazelnut, chestnuts.
Walnuts, almonds and pecans are drupes. The fruit of the coconut is a large, dry drupe. Cashew nuts are not true nuts. In the cashew nut, the ovary develops outside of the fruit (false fruit). The groundnut or peanut is not a true nut, but rather a legume. In groundnut, edible the cotyledons remain below ground and act as a storage organ.

questions.question: A close approximation of an axon of one neuron and a dendrite of the next is called ________.
questions.question_raw: A close approximation of an axon of one neuron and a dendrite of the next is called ________.
questions.explanation: The close connection between an axon and dendrite of the next neuron is called a synapse. Nerve impulses pass through it in the form of neurotransmitters due to the presence of synaptic fluid present in it.

questions.question: Chlorophyll is located inside_______.
questions.question_raw: Chlorophyll is located inside_______.
questions.explanation: The chloroplast consists of a number of organized flattened membranous sacs called the thylakoids, Chlorophyll pigments are present in the thylakoids.

questions.question: ATP is______.
questions.explanation: Adenosine triphosphate (ATP) is a nucleotide. A nucleotide is formed by pentose sugar, nitrogenous base and phosphates. Many nucleotides join together to form polynucleotide.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is not true for plasma membrane?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following statements is not true for plasma membrane?

options.text: Carbohydrate is never found in it.
Lipid is present as a bilayer in it.
It is present in both plant and animal cells.
Proteins are present integrated as well as loosely associated with the lipid bilayer.

correct_option_texts: Carbohydrate is never found in it.
questions.explanation: Carbohydrates are found in the membrane are branched or unbranched oligosaccharides eg hexose, hexosamine, silica acid, etc.

questions.question: The enzymes show high activity at:
questions.question_raw: The enzymes show high activity at:
questions.explanation: Each enzyme shows its highest activity at a particular temperature and pH called the optimum temperature and optimum pH.

questions.question: The structure of the plasma membrane fluid mosaic model is proposed by:
questions.question_raw: The structure of the plasma membrane fluid mosaic model is proposed by:
questions.explanation: The fluid mosaic model explains various observations regarding the structure of functional cell membranes. The model, which was devised by SJ Singer and GL Nicolson in 1972, describes the cell membrane as a two-dimensional liquid that restricts the lateral diffusion of membrane components.

questions.question: The sporophytes bearing sporangia having leaf-like appendages are called ________.
questions.question_raw: The sporophytes bearing sporangia having leaf-like appendages are called ________.
questions.explanation: The sporophytes bear sporangia that are subtended by leaf-like appendages called sporophylls.

questions.explanation: The hierarchical order of taxonomic categories is a sequence of categories in decreasing order is from kingdom to species. The kingdom has the highest rank followed by division, class, order, family, genus, and species in botany.

questions.question_raw: Plants can synthesis all types of fatty acids but animals lack the machinery to synthesis three fatty acids called essential fatty acids. They are:
questions.explanation: Plants can synthesis all types of fatty acids but animal cells lack the machinery to synthesis three fatty acids called essential fatty acids. These are Linoleic, Linolenic, and Arachidonic acids.

questions.explanation: The nucleotide has three chemically distinct components. One is a heterocyclic compound, the second is a monosaccharide and the third a phosphoric acid or phosphate.

options.text: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
A is true but R is false.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is false but R is true.

correct_option_texts: A is false but R is true.
questions.explanation: Cofactor may be inorganic or organic in nature. Organic cofactors are of two types, coenzymes and prosthetic groups. Coenzymes are easily separable nonprotein organic cofactors. Prosthetic groups are nonprotein organic cofactors firmly attached to apoenzymes (protein part of enzyme).

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Living systems have a high degree of tendency for undergoing entropy.
Reason (R): Living systems are unable to overcome entropy.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): Living systems have a high degree of tendency for undergoing entropy.
Reason (R): Living systems are unable to overcome entropy.

options.text: A is true but R is false.
Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
A is false but R is true.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.

correct_option_texts: A is true but R is false.
questions.explanation: Law of entropy states when a living or non living system is left to itself, then it tends to increase its disorder or randomness i.e. entropy. So entropy is the degree of unavailable or useless energy. Living systems carry a number of chemical reactions and physical changes. Since no energy transfer or transformation is cent percent efficient, there is a regular loss of energy, thus bringing entropy. Living systems overcome entropy by continuous input of usable or free energy. The Iiving organism obtain this required free energy either from radiant energy by photosynthesis (eg. plants) or from ready made food (eg. animals).
