questions.explanation: Apocrine glands are a kind of exocrine gland found in the skin, breast, eyelids, and ears. Mammary glands are present in the breast that release milk in mammals.

questions.question:
Which of the following is not correct?
 Robert Brown discovered the cell.
 Schleiden and Schwann formulated the cell theory.
 Virchow explained that cells are formed from pre-existing cells.
 A unicellular organism carries out its life activities within a single cell.
questions.question_raw:
Which of the following is not correct?

  1. Robert Brown discovered the cell.
  2. Schleiden and Schwann formulated the cell theory.
  3. Virchow explained that cells are formed from pre-existing cells.
  4. A unicellular organism carries out its life activities within a single cell.
questions.explanation: Scottish botanist Robert Brown (1773-1858) was responsible for discovering the nucleus of a cell. The cell was first discovered by Robert Hooke in 1665 using a microscope.

questions.question: The following ratio is generally constant for a given species:
questions.question_raw: The following ratio is generally constant for a given species:
questions.explanation: The ratio of the sum of guanine and cytosine to the sum of adenine and thymine is always constant for a species. This is due to the formation of hydrogen bonds between purine and pyrimidine.

questions.question: Largest organelles in the cell are:
questions.question_raw: Largest organelles in the cell are:

options.text: Centrioles in animals and plastids in plant cell
Mitochondria in animal cell and chloroplast in plant cell

correct_option_texts: Mitochondria in animal cell and chloroplast in plant cell
questions.explanation: Mitochondria is the largest cell organelles in animal cell and chloroplast is the largest cell organelles in a plant cell.

questions.question: Which one is the neutral amino acid?
questions.question_raw: Which one is the neutral amino acid?
questions.explanation: Sidechains that have pure hydrocarbon alkyl groups (alkane branches) or aromatic (benzene rings) are non-polar. Examples include valine, alanine, leucine, isoleucine, phenylalanine.

mock_tests.instruction:
The Question Paper contains three sections.
 Section A has 24 questions. Attempt any 20 questions.
 Section B has 24 questions. Attempt any 20 questions.
 Section C has 12 questions. Attempt any 10 questions.
 All questions carry equal marks.
 There is no negative marking.

questions.question: It is a handy book containing instructions as to the occurrence, collection, and identification of species found in a particular area.
questions.question_raw: It is a handy book containing instructions as to the occurrence, collection, and identification of species found in a particular area.
questions.explanation: Manual is a handy book containing instructions as to the occurrence, collection, and identification of species found in a particular area. It serves as an important tool for the classification of organisms.

questions.question: Which of the following plastids stores oil and fat?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following plastids stores oil and fat?
questions.explanation: Elaioplasts are a type of leucoplast that is specialized for the storage of lipids in plants. Elaioplasts house oil body deposits as rounded plastoglobuli, which are essentially fat droplets.

questions.question: Laminaria and Fucus belong to:
questions.question_raw: Laminaria and Fucus belong to:
questions.explanation: The common forms of Brown algae or Phaeophyceae are Ectocarpus, Dictyota, Laminaria, Sargassum and Fucus.

questions.question: Which phyla does not possess a coelom?
questions.question_raw: Which phyla does not possess a coelom?
questions.explanation: Phylum Platyhelminthes does not possess a coelom. The coelom is the body cavity in between organs. Animals with coelom are called coelomate and those without it are called acoelomate.

questions.question: A bacterium capable of tolerating extreme heat, dryness, and toxic chemicals is probably having_____.
questions.question_raw: A bacterium capable of tolerating extreme heat, dryness, and toxic chemicals is probably having_____.
questions.explanation: A bacterium capable of tolerating extreme heat, dryness, and toxic chemicals have the ability to produce endospores inside the body that help them to tide over unfavorable conditions.

questions.question: What is the name of muscle fibres?
questions.question_raw: What is the name of muscle fibres?
questions.explanation: Muscle tissue is made of elongated fibre-like structures called Myofibrils. Each myofibril is made of actin and myosin filament.

questions.question: Golgi apparatus is the site for the formation of:
questions.question_raw: Golgi apparatus is the site for the formation of:
questions.explanation: Golgi apparatus is the important site of formation of glycoproteins and glycolipids.

questions.question: Polysaccharide does not metabolise in the human body and used for kidney function is________.
questions.question_raw: Polysaccharide does not metabolise in the human body and used for kidney function is________.
questions.explanation: Inulin is a starchy substance found in a wide variety of fruits, vegetables and other herbs. This polysaccharide is not metabolized in the human body and used for kidney function.

questions.question: The eukaryotic ribosomes are of_____.
questions.question_raw: The eukaryotic ribosomes are of_____.
questions.explanation: The eukaryotic ribosomes are the 80S while the prokaryotic ribosomes are 70S. Here ‘S’ stands for the sedimentation coefficient; it indirectly is a measure of density and size.

questions.question: A polysaccharide present as a storehouse of the energy of plant tissues:
questions.question_raw: A polysaccharide present as a storehouse of the energy of plant tissues:
questions.explanation: Starch is a variant of this but present as a storehouse of energy in plant tissues.

questions.question: A true species consist of a population:
questions.question_raw: A true species consist of a population:
questions.explanation:
As per the definition given by Ernst Mayr in 1964: "A species is a group of actually or potentially inbreeding populations that are reproductively isolated from other such groups."
A species may have subgroups, called subspecies or varieties, showing certain distinct features of their own.

questions.question: The diagram represents
questions.question_raw: The diagram represents
questions.explanation: The diagram shown is a uracil which is a pyramidine belonging to nitrogen bases.

questions.question: All seed-bearing plant, alternation of generation is_________.
questions.question_raw: All seed-bearing plant, alternation of generation is_________.
questions.explanation: All seed-bearing plants i.e., gymnosperms and angiosperms follow diplontic alternation of a generation where the diploid sporophyte is the dominant, photosynthetic, independent phase of the plant. The gametophytic phase is represented by the single to the few-celled haploid gametophyte.

questions.question: Megasporangium of seed plants are called:
questions.question_raw: Megasporangium of seed plants are called:
questions.explanation: Megasporangium of seed plants is called an ovule. The ovule is present inside the ovary which becomes seed on maturation after fertilization. It produces megasporocytes or Megaspore mother cells that yield megaspores.

questions.question: Which one is important in nutrient to recycle and act as decomposer and mineralizes of the biosphere ______.
questions.question_raw: Which one is important in nutrient to recycle and act as decomposer and mineralizes of the biosphere ______.
questions.explanation: Fungi is important in the nutrient cycle and acts as decomposer along with bacteria and mineralizers of the biosphere to regular recycling of nutrients in the biosphere.

questions.question:
Match the following and choose the correct answer with reference to cockroach:
 Column A
 Column B
 (i) Bundles of sperm
 (a) Ovarioles
 (ii) External genitalia
 (b) Gonopore
 (iii) Chain of developing ova
 (c) Phallomere
 (iv) Opening of ejaculatory duct
 (d) Spermatophore
questions.question_raw:
Match the following and choose the correct answer with reference to cockroach:

Column A Column B
(i) Bundles of sperm (a) Ovarioles
(ii) External genitalia (b) Gonopore
(iii) Chain of developing ova (c) Phallomere
(iv) Opening of ejaculatory duct (d) Spermatophore
questions.explanation:
The sperms are stored in the seminal vesicles and are glued together in the form of bundles called spermatophores which are discharged during copulation.
 The external genitalia is represented by chitinous asymmetrical structures surrounding the male gonopore is called male gonapophysis or phallomere.
 Each ovary is formed of a group of eight ovarian tubules or ovarioles, containing a chain of developing ova. 
 The ejaculatory duct opens into a male gonopore situated ventral to the anus.

questions.question: Which organelle is not found in plant cells?
questions.question_raw: Which organelle is not found in plant cells?
questions.explanation: Centriole (Helps in cell division by forming spindle fibre) is present only in animal cells.

questions.question: The amino acids are linked together serially by:
questions.question_raw: The amino acids are linked together serially by:
questions.explanation: The amino acids are linked together one after the other by peptide bonds. This bond is formed between NH_3 of one amino acid with a carboxyl end of another amino acid to release water.

questions.question: Why are biological names written in Italics?
questions.question_raw: Why are biological names written in Italics?
questions.explanation: At the time when biologist Carl Linnaeus (1707-1778) published the books that are now accepted as the starting point of binomial nomenclature, Latin was used in Western Europe as the common language of science, and scientific names were in Latin.

questions.question: The epithelium involved in diffusion is:
questions.question_raw: The epithelium involved in diffusion is:
questions.explanation: The squamous epithelium is made of a single thin layer of flattened cells with irregular boundaries. They are found in the walls of blood vessels and air sacs of the lungs and are involved in functions like forming a diffusion boundary.

questions.question: Which one is peat moss?
questions.question_raw: Which one is peat moss?
questions.explanation: Sphagnum is also known as peat moss as it provides peat that has long been used as fuel.

questions.question: In Coelenterates, the cell shows:
questions.question_raw: In Coelenterates, the cell shows:
questions.explanation: In coelenterates, the arrangement of cells is more complex. Here, the cells performing the same function are arranged into tissues, hence is called tissue level of organization.

questions.question: The plant cells differ from animal cells in:
questions.question_raw: The plant cells differ from animal cells in:
questions.explanation: The plant cells have a eukaryotic structure with prominent chloroplasts and cell walls mainly made of cellulose.

questions.question: In water, frogs breathe through the skin. What is the name for such kind of respiration?
questions.question_raw: In water, frogs breathe through the skin. What is the name for such kind of respiration?
questions.explanation: In Cutaneous respiration, the exchange of gases occurs through the skin. Animals undergoing cutaneous respiration usually have moist skin.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Secondary metabolites are produced in small quantities and their extraction from the plant is difficult and expensive.
Reason (R): Secondary metabolites can be commercially produced by using tissue culture technique.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): Secondary metabolites are produced in small quantities and their extraction from the plant is difficult and expensive.
Reason (R): Secondary metabolites can be commercially produced by using tissue culture technique.

options.text: A is false but R is true.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
A is true but R is false.

correct_option_texts: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
questions.explanation: Secondary metabolites are biosynthetically derived from primary metabolites but are more limited in distribution in plant kingdoms being restricted to a particular taxonomic group. By culture media using tissue culture technique, secondary metabolites can be produced on a large scale.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): The science of classifiying organisms is called taxonomy.
Reason (R): Systematics and taxonomy have same meaning.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): The science of classifiying organisms is called taxonomy.
Reason (R): Systematics and taxonomy have same meaning.

options.text: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is true but R is false.
A is false but R is true.

correct_option_texts: A is true but R is false.
questions.explanation: Taxonomy is the science of identification, nomenclature and classification of organisms. But taxonomy and systematics are different terms. Systematics is the branch of biology that deals with the diversity of organisms at every level of classification.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Cell is an open system.
Reason (R): Cell receives a number of materials including energy-containing nutrients from outside.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): Cell is an open system.
Reason (R): Cell receives a number of materials including energy-containing nutrients from outside.

options.text: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
A is true but R is false.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is false but R is true.

correct_option_texts: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
questions.explanation: The cell is an isothermal open system as all the parts of the cell at any given time maintains the same temperature and pressure. The cell is an open system in which materials and energy are transferred between organisms and the exterior environment.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Food reserves of algae vary from group to group.
Reason (R): Algae are autotrophic in nature.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): Food reserves of algae vary from group to group.
Reason (R): Algae are autotrophic in nature.

options.text: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
A is false but R is true.
A is true but R is false.

correct_option_texts: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
questions.explanation: With respect to their nutrition the algae are autotrophic. All most of the cells of the thallus normally contain chlorophyll. The green cells can manufacture their own carbohydrate food from CO_2 and water through the agency of sunlight. Since the early steps in photosynthesis in all the algal groups are practically the same, the food materials which accumulate as food reserves is in the form of polysaccharides, however, vary from group to group and thus provide useful data for preliminary classification of algae.

questions.question: The largest phylum of kingdom animalia is ________.
questions.question_raw: The largest phylum of kingdom animalia is ________.
questions.explanation: Arthropoda is the largest phylum of kingdom Animalia. It includes all insects, arachnids, myriapods, and crustaceans. They are characterized by jointed limbs and cuticles.

questions.question: A plant that is partially heterotrophic is______.
questions.question_raw: A plant that is partially heterotrophic is______.
questions.explanation: Drosera plant is partially heterotrophic. These plants capture insects to fulfil its protein requirement although it performs photosynthesis also.

questions.question: The epithelium found in the lining of the stomach and intestine is ________.
questions.question_raw: The epithelium found in the lining of the stomach and intestine is ________.
questions.explanation: The columnar epithelium is found in the lining of the stomach and intestine and help in secretion and absorption. It also provides a barrier against harmful microorganisms.

questions.question: Given graph shows the effect of substrate concentration on the rate of reaction of enzyme phosphatase. What does given graph indicate?
questions.question_raw: Given graph shows the effect of substrate concentration on the rate of reaction of enzyme phosphatase. What does given graph indicate?

options.text: Rate is proportional to substrate concentration
At high substrate concentration pH changes

questions.explanation: The graph showed above shows effects of substrate concentration on rate of reaction of enzyme phosphate. Usually rate of reaction increases with increase in concentration but presence of enzyme inhibitors decrease the velocity.

questions.question: A group of plants with similar traits of any rank is _______.
questions.question_raw: A group of plants with similar traits of any rank is _______.
questions.explanation: Each category in the taxonomic hierarchy represents the rank and is commonly termed as a taxon. Hence, the correct option is Taxon.

questions.question: The chromatin contains some basic proteins are called as ________.
questions.question_raw: The chromatin contains some basic proteins are called as ________.
questions.explanation: Histone proteins the basic packer and arranger of chromatin.

questions.question: The structure formed by germinated fern spore is:
questions.question_raw: The structure formed by germinated fern spore is:
questions.explanation: Fern spores germinate to give rise to inconspicuous, small but multicellular, free-living, mostly photosynthetic thalloid gametophytes called prothallus. The gametophytes bear male and female sex organs called antheridia and archegonia.

questions.question: What is the main excretory organ in insects?
questions.question_raw: What is the main excretory organ in insects?
questions.explanation: The insects belong to phylum Arthropoda and hence, the excretory organ is Malphighian tubules.

questions.question: A distinction between unicellular and multicellular is not possible in case of
questions.question_raw: A distinction between unicellular and multicellular is not possible in case of
questions.explanation: Algae may be unicellular as well as multicellular, primitive algae are unicellular, and red and green algae are multicellular having a length of several meters. It is not possible to distinguish unicellular and multicellular algae because of this that unicellular green algae have not been included in kingdom Protista by Whittaker.

questions.question: Which one is a heterocrine gland?
questions.question_raw: Which one is a heterocrine gland?
questions.explanation: The pancreas is called heterocrine gland because it acts as both endocrine and exocrine gland to secrete hormone as well as digestive enzymes. Testis and Ovary are also heterocrine glands.

questions.question: Lipid molecules of plasma membrane occur
questions.question_raw: Lipid molecules of plasma membrane occur
questions.explanation: Phospholipids found in the plasma membrane is a lipid bilayer having a hydrophilic region called the head and a hydrophobic region called the tail. The hydrophilic region can interact with water due to the presence of a phosphate group which is polar, like water. The membrane is in contact with water on both the inner and outer sides. The hydrophilic heads arrange automatically on the outer side of the bilayer due to polarity and hydrophobic heads are facing each other between the bilayer, thus arranging all lipid molecules parallel to each other.

questions.question: Most abundant mineral in body fluids are:
questions.question_raw: Most abundant mineral in body fluids are:
questions.explanation: Most abundant mineral in body fluids is Na^+ and Cl^- ions. These ions are responsible for the generation of impulses and its transfer through neuron cells.

questions.question: Which of the following suffixes used for units of classification in plants indicates a taxonomic category of family.
questions.question_raw: Which of the following suffixes used for units of classification in plants indicates a taxonomic category of family.
questions.explanation: You can consider the names of families, e.g. Solanaceae, fabaceae, etc.

questions.question: Which of the following organisms were placed together under algae?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following organisms were placed together under algae?
questions.explanation: As spirogyra, chlorella and chlamydomonas have same characterstics which are perfectly suitable for the 'algae', they all are placed together in algae.

questions.question: Cardiac muscles are:
questions.question_raw: Cardiac muscles are:
questions.explanation: Cardiac muscle tissue is a contractile tissue present only in the heart. The cardiac muscle fibres are elongated, branched and uninucleate. They appear striated due to the presence of alternate light and dark band. Cardiac muscles are self-contracting and involuntary in nature. The contract in a rhythmic fashion and help the heart to pump the blood.

questions.question: A specified area in which multiple uses of the land is permitted by dividing into zones, each for a particular activity is called ________.
questions.question_raw: A specified area in which multiple uses of the land is permitted by dividing into zones, each for a particular activity is called ________.
questions.explanation: A biosphere reserve is divided into a number of zones, where different kinds of activities are allowed. In the outer zone, agriculture and other activities are allowed but in the central or core zone, no human activities are allowed.

questions.question: The stain used to visualise motochondria is:
questions.question_raw: The stain used to visualise motochondria is:
questions.explanation: Janus green is used to stain mitochondria.

questions.question: Asexual reproduction in liverworts takes place by specialised structures, commonly known as:
questions.question_raw: Asexual reproduction in liverworts takes place by specialised structures, commonly known as:
questions.explanation: Asexual reproduction in liverworts takes place by the formation of specialized structures called gemmae (sing. gemma). Gemmae are green, multicellular, asexual buds, which develop in small receptacles called gemma cups located on the thalli. The gemmae become detached from the parent body and germinate to form new individuals.

questions.question: Choose the odd one out based on coelom: Annelids, Molluscs, Platyhelminthes, Arthropods.
questions.question_raw: Choose the odd one out based on coelom: Annelids, Molluscs, Platyhelminthes, Arthropods.
questions.explanation: Animals possessing coelom are called coelomates such as annelids, molluscs, arthropods, echinoderms, hemichordates and chordates. In Platyhelmites coelom is absent.

questions.question: The epithelium found in proximal convoluted tubule (PCT) of the nephron in the kidney is_______.
questions.question_raw: The epithelium found in proximal convoluted tubule (PCT) of the nephron in the kidney is_______.
questions.explanation: The cuboidal epithelium is composed of a single layer of cube-like cells found in ducts of glands and tubular parts of nephrons in kidneys and its main functions are secretion and absorption.

Instructions for Question 49 to 54:
Each flower normally has four floral whorls, viz., calyx, corolla, androecium and gynoecium. The calyx is the outermost whorl of the flower and the members are called sepals. Corolla is composed of petals. Petals are usually brightly coloured to attract insects for pollination. The mode of arrangement of sepals or petals in floral bud with respect to the other members of the same whorl is known as aestivation. The main types of aestivation are valvate, twisted, imbricate. The shape and colour of corolla vary greatly in plants. Corolla may be tubular, bell- shaped, funnel-shaped or wheel-shaped and vexillary.

questions.question: The Aleurone layer of Maize grain is especially rich in_________.
questions.question_raw: The Aleurone layer of Maize grain is especially rich in_________.
questions.explanation: Aleurone layer of maize grain is rich in proteins. It is the outermost layer of the endosperm followed by the inner starchy endosperm.

questions.question: Two animal cells are interconnected by:
questions.question_raw: Two animal cells are interconnected by:
questions.explanation: Two animal cells are interconnected by desmosomes, through which the exchange of materials takes place between two cells. It is formed from protein plaques in the cell membranes linked by filaments.

questions.question: Which of the following is not true of a eukaryotic cell?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following is not true of a eukaryotic cell?

options.text: Cell wall is made up of peptidoglycans.
80S type of ribosomes are present in the cytoplasm.

correct_option_texts: Cell wall is made up of peptidoglycans.
questions.explanation: Cell wall is made up of peptidoglycans in case of prokaryotic cells mainly bacterial cell.

questions.question: Secondary plant products are of great use in commerce in making:
questions.question_raw: Secondary plant products are of great use in commerce in making:
questions.explanation: Many secondary plant products are of great use in commerce in making drug and perfumes. The primary product of plants is carbohydrates synthesized during photosynthesis.

questions.question: Which organelle is also known as a suicide bag of the cell?
questions.question_raw: Which organelle is also known as a suicide bag of the cell?
questions.explanation: Lysosomes are membrane-enclosed organelles that contain an array of enzymes capable of breaking down all types of biological polymers. One of its functions is to digest materials taken up from outside the cell. That process is called endocytosis. That's why it is called the Suicidal Bag of the cell.

questions.question: Which one is an initiation codon for protein synthesis?
questions.question_raw: Which one is an initiation codon for protein synthesis?
questions.explanation: AUG codon is an initiation codon for protein synthesis. This codon code for amino acids methionine.

mock_tests.instruction:
The Question Paper contains three sections.
 Section A has 24 questions. Attempt any 20 questions.
 Section B has 24 questions. Attempt any 20 questions.
 Section C has 12 questions. Attempt any 10 questions.
 All questions carry equal marks.
 There is no negative marking.

questions.question: The term systematics refers to:
questions.question_raw: The term systematics refers to:

options.text: Identification and preservation of plants and animals
Identification and study of organ systems
Diversity of kinds of organisms and their relationship
Study of habitats of organisms and their classification

correct_option_texts: Diversity of kinds of organisms and their relationship
questions.explanation: In fact, the need for a proper classification system was felt because of huge diversity of organisms. Based on certain relationships, organisms were classified into different group and sub-groups.

questions.question: Which one of the following is common between Prokaryotic and Eukaryotic cell?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following is common between Prokaryotic and Eukaryotic cell?
questions.explanation: Most prokaryotic cells, particularly the bacterial cells, have a chemically complex cell envelope. All eukaryotic cells are not identical. Plant and animal cells are different as the former possess cell walls.

questions.question: The sperms of ________ are largest in the biological world.
questions.question_raw: The sperms of ________ are largest in the biological world.
questions.explanation: The sperms of male gamete of Cycas are the largest in the biological world. It is top-shaped. In Cycas revoluta the size of the sperm ranges between 180 to 210 μm and they are clearly visible to the naked eye.

questions.question: Crocodile and penguin are similar to whale and dogfish in which one of the following features?
questions.question_raw: Crocodile and penguin are similar to whale and dogfish in which one of the following features?

options.text: Lay eggs and guard them till they hatch
Have gill slit at some stage of life

correct_option_texts: Have gill slit at some stage of life
questions.explanation: Crocodile and penguins are similar to whale and dogfish in having gill slits at some stage of their life cycle which is used as respiratory organs.

questions.question: Two categories of animals on the basis of red blood are:
questions.question_raw: Two categories of animals on the basis of red blood are:
questions.explanation: Haemoglobin in RBCs imparts red colour to it. The organisms whose blood contains haemoglobin are called enaima and those without it are called anaima.

questions.question: What type of metamorphosis occurs in cockroach?
questions.question_raw: What type of metamorphosis occurs in cockroach?
questions.explanation: The development of cockroach is paurometabolous. This means a gradual development from the nymphal stage to the adult stage. Nymph represents a miniature adult and lives in the same habitat.

questions.question: Arrangement of ciliary microtubules is:
questions.question_raw: Arrangement of ciliary microtubules is:
questions.explanation: Cilia present in prokaryotic organisms consist of microtubules. These microtubules are arranged in 9+2 order in which 2 forms the centre and 9 are arranged at the periphery.

questions.question: Element located in the centre of the porphyrin ring of chlorophyll is ______.
questions.question_raw: Element located in the centre of the porphyrin ring of chlorophyll is ______.
questions.explanation: Chlorophyll molecule consists of a porphyrin ring and a long tail like a chain of carbon and hydrogen. The centre of the porphyrin ring is occupied by Mg ions.

questions.question: The stacks of closely packed thylakoids are called ________.
questions.question_raw: The stacks of closely packed thylakoids are called ________.
questions.explanation: Granum: A stacked membranous structure within the chloroplasts of plants and green algae that contains chlorophyll and is the site of the light reactions of photosynthesis. The saclike membranes that makeup grana are known as thylakoids.

questions.question: The protein portion of an enzyme is called:
questions.question_raw: The protein portion of an enzyme is called:
questions.explanation: Apoenzyme: The polypeptide or protein part of the enzyme is called the apoenzyme and may be inactive in its original synthesized structure. The inactive form of the apoenzyme is known as a proenzyme or zymogen.

questions.question: A living organism is unexceptionally differentiated from a non-living structure on the basis of
questions.question_raw: A living organism is unexceptionally differentiated from a non-living structure on the basis of
questions.explanation: All organisms, from primitive prokaryotes to most advanced and complex eukaryotes, are able to sense and respond to environmental factors. The stimuli are perceived by sense organs in higher animals through sensory receptors e.g., eyes, ears, nose. Plants do not possess such sense organs. However, they do respond to external factors such as light, water, temperature, pollutants, other organisms, etc. Human beings have an additional facility of self-consciousness (awareness of self). Consciousness and response to stimuli are said to be the defining properties of living organisms.

questions.question: Identify the correct pairing between nitrogen bases in DNA.
questions.question_raw: Identify the correct pairing between nitrogen bases in DNA.
questions.explanation: There are two hydrogen bonds between A and T pairing. There are three hydrogen bonds between G and C pairing. Each strand appears like a helical staircase.

questions.question: Protonema is:
questions.question_raw: Protonema is:

options.text: Diploid and is found in pteridophytes
Haploid and is found in pteridophytes
Haploid and is found in mosses
Diploid and is found in liverworts

correct_option_texts: Haploid and is found in mosses
questions.explanation: A protenemal stage is a juvenile or tentative stage in the life history of mosses. They are haploid in nature. It arises after the germination of spore. It is short-lived.

questions.question: On germination, moss spore produce _________.
questions.question_raw: On germination, moss spore produce _________.
questions.explanation: The spore of moss germinates to produce protonema. It is a creeping, green, branched, and frequently filamentous stage, which develops into secondary protonema as a lateral bud. This stage bears sex organs.

questions.question: Which bacteria help in oxidising inorganic substances?
questions.question_raw: Which bacteria help in oxidising inorganic substances?
questions.explanation: Chemosynthetic autotrophic bacteria oxidize various inorganic substances such as nitrates, nitrites, and ammonia and use the released energy for their ATP production. They play a great role in recycling nutrients like nitrogen, phosphorous, iron, and sulphur.

questions.question: Antibodies are formed by:
questions.question_raw: Antibodies are formed by:
questions.explanation: Antibodies are formed against any foreign body entering the body. It is formed by plasma cells, globulins that develop resistance against a particular type of disease.

questions.question: New cells generate from
questions.question_raw: New cells generate from

questions.question: Blood concentration of glucose in a normal healthy individual is:
questions.question_raw: Blood concentration of glucose in a normal healthy individual is:
questions.explanation: The blood concentration of glucose in a normal healthy individual is 4.5-5.0 mM.

questions.question_raw: As we go from species to kingdom in a taxonomic hierarchy, the number of common characteristics
questions.explanation: Number of common characters is maximum among members of a particular species. On the other hand, number of common characters is minimum among members of a particular kingdom.

 Column A
 Column B

Match the following with reference to the frog:

Column A Column B
(i) Medulla oblongata (a) Sensory papillae

The medulla oblongata passes out through the foramen magnum and continues into the spinal cord, which is enclosed in the vertebral column.

questions.question: Which of the following is also known as the amphibian of the plant kingdom?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following is also known as the amphibian of the plant kingdom?
questions.explanation: Bryophytes are also called amphibians of the plant kingdom because these plants can live in soil but are dependent on water for sexual reproduction.

questions.question: The excretory organ of Saccoglossus is:
questions.question_raw: The excretory organ of Saccoglossus is:
questions.explanation: Saccoglossus belong to phylum Hemichordata and hence the excretory organ is proboscis gland.

questions.question: How many times do nymphs moult to reach the adult form of a cockroach?

questions.explanation: When the ootheca hatches, a tiny nymph comes out. It grows by undergoing molting about 13 times to reach the adult form.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Proteins are a heteropolymer.
Reason (R): Dietary proteins are the source of non-essential amino acids.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): Proteins are a heteropolymer.
Reason (R): Dietary proteins are the source of non-essential amino acids.

options.text: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
A is true but R is false.
A is false but R is true.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.

correct_option_texts: A is true but R is false.
questions.explanation: Each individual protein is a polymer of amino acids. As there are 20 types of amino acids, a protein is a heteropolymer and not a homopolymer. Amino acids can be essential or non-essential. Certain amino acids are essential for our health and they have to be supplied through our diet. Dietary proteins are thus, a source of essential amino acids. Non-essential amino acids are those amino acids that are synthesized in our bodies.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Fertilisers improve the crop production.
Reason (R): Fertilisers are pollutants.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): Fertilisers improve the crop production.
Reason (R): Fertilisers are pollutants.

options.text: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
A is true but R is false.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is false but R is true.

correct_option_texts: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
questions.explanation: Most of the soil are rich in the element required for the proper growth of the plant. But few of them are however deficient in certain elements. Moreover constant agricultural cultivation in the field may also cause depletion of certain elements which must be replenished in order to improve fertility of soil. Fertilizers contain nitrogen, potassium and phosphorous which generally become deficient in soil. Thus fertilisers are used to increase crop yield. They have become water pollutants. Some of the fertilisers have reached ground water and cause malfunctioning in human beings, especially infants.

questions.question:

Reason (R): The cytoplasm is a semi-fluid matrix.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): The cytoplasm is the main arena of cellular activities in both the plant and animal cells.

options.text: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is false but R is true.
A is true but R is false.
Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.

correct_option_texts: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
questions.explanation: In both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells, a semi-fluid matrix called cytoplasm occupies the volume of the cell. The cytoplasm is the main arena of cellular activities in both plant and animal cells. Various chemical reactions occur in it to keep the cell in the ‘living state’.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Zygospore of Ulothrix forms meiozoospores and meioa-planospores.
Reason (R): Both are motile.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): Zygospore of Ulothrix forms meiozoospores and meioa-planospores.
Reason (R): Both are motile.

options.text: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
A is true but R is false.
A is false but R is true.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.

correct_option_texts: A is true but R is false.
questions.explanation: The zygospore of Ulothrix, on germination, does not directly develop into a new filament. Instead its nucleus undergoes zygotic meiosis. The resultant four haploid daughter nuclei are organised into meiospores which may be motile (meiozoospores) or non-motile (meioaplanospores).

questions.question: Petromyzon is included under_______.
questions.question_raw: Petromyzon is included under_______.
questions.explanation: Petromyzon is a jawless vertebrate and hence included under Agnatha.

questions.explanation: The Nilgiri Biosphere reserve is the first biosphere reserve established in India. Biosphere reserves are set to protect the flora and fauna of a particular area that houses a large number of wild animals.

questions.explanation: The vacuole is bound by a single membrane called tonoplast. In-plant cells the vacuoles can occupy up to 90% of the volume of the cell. In plants, the tonoplast facilitates the transport of a number of ions.

questions.explanation: Most of the algae are aquatic, can be freshwater algae - Chlorophyceae or marine algae - Phaeophyceae and Rhodophyceae.

questions.question_raw: A marine cartilaginous fish that can produce electric current is:
questions.explanation: Torpedo is a genus of rays, commonly known as electric rays, torpedo rays, or torpedoes. They are slow-moving bottom-dwellers capable of generating electricity as a defence and feeding mechanism.

questions.question: ______ is an example of a fungal infection.
questions.question_raw: ______ is an example of a fungal infection.
questions.explanation: Ringworm, also known as dermatophytosis or tinea, is a fungal infection of the skin. The name “ringworm” is a misnomer since the infection is caused by a fungus, not a worm. Ringworm infection can affect both humans and animals.

questions.question_raw: The type of connective tissue that is associated with the umbilical cord is:
questions.explanation: Jelly like connective tissue is associated with the umbilical cord. It is a flexible cord-like structure containing blood vessels and attaching a human fetus to the placenta during gestation.

questions.explanation: Proteins human haemoglobin consists of 4 subunits. Two of these are identical to each other. Hence, two subunits of $\alpha $ type and two subunits of $\beta $ type together constitute the human haemoglobin (Hb).

questions.question: Wildlife Protection Act 1972 is for:
questions.question_raw: Wildlife Protection Act 1972 is for:

options.text: Preventing the killing and capturing of wildlife

correct_option_texts: Preventing the killing and capturing of wildlife

questions.question_raw: Sexual reproduction is by the population of male and female followed by embryological development is a characteristic feature of_______.
questions.explanation: The sexual reproduction in the kingdom Animalia is by the population of males and female followed by embryological development.

questions.question: The muscle which is immune to fatigue are:
questions.question_raw: The muscle which is immune to fatigue are:
questions.explanation: The muscles which are immune to fatigue are cardiac muscles present exclusively in the heart. They work from birth till death without getting fatigued.

questions.question: Lions are found in:

questions.explanation: Epiphytes are dependent on other large plants for space or physical support. It remains to attach with other plants but does not obtain food or another nutrient from it so they are not parasitic on the supporting plants.

questions.question_raw: Which phylum has bioluminescence as its well-marked feature?

questions.question: Nerve fibres in the CNS can regenerate after injury due to the presence of________.
questions.question_raw: Nerve fibres in the CNS can regenerate after injury due to the presence of________.
questions.explanation: Nerve fibres in the CNS can regenerate after injury due to the presence of neurilemma. They are the outermost nucleated cytoplasmic layer of Schwann cells that surrounds the axon of the neuron.

questions.question:
Instructions for Question 49 to 54:
Each flower normally has four floral whorls, viz., calyx, corolla, androecium and gynoecium. The gynoecium is the female reproductive part of the flower and is made up of one or more carpels. A carpel consists of three parts namely stigma, style and ovary. The ovary is the enlarged basal part. The style connects the ovary to the stigma. The stigma is usually at the tip of the style and is the receptive surface for pollen grains. After fertilisation, the ovules develop into seeds and the ovary matures into a fruit. The arrangement of ovules within the ovary is known as placentation. The placentation are of different types namely, marginal, axile, parietal, basal, central and free central.
Select the odd one out.

 Ovary is the enlarged basal part, on which lies the elongated tube, the stigma.
 After fertilisation, the ovules develop into seeds and the ovary matures into a fruit.
questions.question_raw:
Instructions for Question 49 to 54:

Each flower normally has four floral whorls, viz., calyx, corolla, androecium and gynoecium. The gynoecium is the female reproductive part of the flower and is made up of one or more carpels. A carpel consists of three parts namely stigma, style and ovary. The ovary is the enlarged basal part. The style connects the ovary to the stigma. The stigma is usually at the tip of the style and is the receptive surface for pollen grains. After fertilisation, the ovules develop into seeds and the ovary matures into a fruit. The arrangement of ovules within the ovary is known as placentation. The placentation are of different types namely, marginal, axile, parietal, basal, central and free central.


Which of the following statements is incorrect?

  1. Each ovary bears one or more ovules attached to a flattened, cushion-like placenta.
  2. In mustard one carpel is present which may be free.
  3. Ovary is the enlarged basal part, on which lies the elongated tube, the stigma.
  4. After fertilisation, the ovules develop into seeds and the ovary matures into a fruit.
questions.explanation: II and III

questions.question: Bat is member of an order________.
questions.question_raw: Bat is member of an order________.
questions.explanation: Bat is a member of the order Chiroptera of class Mammalia. Bats are only mammals to have achieved powered flight in which forelimb is modified as wings.

questions.question: Frog shows which kind of excretion?
questions.question_raw: Frog shows which kind of excretion?

options.text: Ammonotelic in water and ureotelic on land

questions.explanation: Excretion of urea as metabolic waste is known as Ureotelism. Animals secreting urea are called ureotelic. Frog is ureotelic.

questions.question: Which is not a function of the vacuole in a plant cell?
questions.question_raw: Which is not a function of the vacuole in a plant cell?
questions.explanation: Plant cell contains large central vacuole which helps in waste disposal, cell elongation and storage but not involved in the formation of H_2O_2.

questions.question: The oils have:
questions.question_raw: The oils have:
questions.explanation: Oils have a lower melting point (e.g., gingely oil) and hence remain as oil in winters.

questions.question: The bacterial flagellum consists of
questions.question_raw: The bacterial flagellum consists of
questions.explanation:
A bacterial flagellum has 3 basic parts: a filament, a hook, and a basal body.
The filament is the rigid, helical structure that extends from the cell surface.
The hook is a flexible coupling between the filament and the basal body.
The basal body consists of a rod and a series of rings that anchor the flagellum to the cell wall and the cytoplasmic membrane.

questions.question: The chemical compounds undergo changes in shape without the breakdown of bonds are called as:
questions.question_raw: The chemical compounds undergo changes in shape without the breakdown of bonds are called as:
questions.explanation: Chemical compounds undergo two types of changes. A physical change simply refers to a change in shape without breaking of bonds. This is a physical process.

mock_tests.instruction:
The Question Paper contains three sections.
 Section A has 24 questions. Attempt any 20 questions.
 Section B has 24 questions. Attempt any 20 questions.
 Section C has 12 questions. Attempt any 10 questions.
 All questions carry equal marks.
 There is no negative marking.

questions.question: Which are the terms interchangeable?
questions.question_raw: Which are the terms interchangeable?
questions.explanation: The characterization, identification, classification, and nomenclature are the processes that are basic to taxonomy as well as systematics. Hence, the correct option is Taxonomy and systematics.

questions.question: Lysosomes are produced by:
questions.question_raw: Lysosomes are produced by:
questions.explanation: Lysosomes are manufactured and budded into the cytoplasm by the Golgi apparatus with enzymes inside. The enzymes that are within the lysosome are made in the rough endoplasmic reticulum, which is then delivered to the Golgi apparatus via transport vesicles.

questions.question: The embryo sac of an Angiosperm is made up of:
questions.question_raw: The embryo sac of an Angiosperm is made up of:
questions.explanation: Female gametophytic or embryo sac of angiosperm develop up to 8 nuclear, 7 celled prior to fertilization. There are 3 celled egg apparatus ( one egg cell and two synergids), three antipodal cells, and two polar nuclei. The two polar nuclei fuse to form a diploid secondary nucleus.

questions.question: A reptile has a four-chambered heart is:
questions.question_raw: A reptile has a four-chambered heart is:
questions.explanation: Reptiles generally possess three-chamber heart, two upper chamber and single lower chamber. But in crocodile, the heart is four-chambered having two atrium and two ventricles.

questions.question: Identify the cuboidal virus from the following:
questions.question_raw: Identify the cuboidal virus from the following:
questions.explanation: A virus consists of genetic material (DNA or RNA) surrounded by protein capsules. They are of different shapes and sizes. The poliomyelitis virus is cubical in shape.

questions.question: Which one of the following is the correct pairing of a body part and muscle fibre that moves it?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following is the correct pairing of a body part and muscle fibre that moves it?

options.text: Biceps of upper arm - smooth muscle fibres

questions.explanation: The iris contains involuntary smooth muscles. It encircles the pupil of the iris and helps to dilate the pupil when there is insufficient light for the normal function of the eye.

questions.question: Which of the following plastids stores both oil and fat?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following plastids stores both oil and fat?
questions.explanation: Elaioplasts are a type of leucoplast that is specialized for the storage of lipids in plants. Elaioplasts house oil body deposits as rounded plastoglobuli, which are essentially fat droplets.

questions.question: Which of the following defines the living state?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following defines the living state?
questions.explanation: The living organisms in their living state are not in equilibrium because systems at equilibrium cannot perform work. As living organisms work continuously, they cannot afford to reach equilibrium.

questions.question: SER differ from RER in:
questions.question_raw: SER differ from RER in:
questions.explanation: Smooth endoplasmic reticulum (SER) differs from the rough endoplasmic reticulum (RER) in absence of ribosome on the surface of the endoplasmic reticulum.

questions.question: Length of DNA with 23 base pairs is ________.
questions.question_raw: Length of DNA with 23 base pairs is ________.
questions.explanation: The distance between two base pairs in DNA is 3.4 A^o. The length of DNA with 23 base pairs is 23 $\times$ 3.4 = 78.2 A^o.

questions.question: The expansion of ICZN is:
questions.question_raw: The expansion of ICZN is:
questions.explanation: Animal taxonomists have evolved the International Code of Zoological Nomenclature (ICZN). Hence, the correct option is the International code of zoological nomenclature.

questions.question: What name is given to that part of the enzyme where catalytic work is carried out?
questions.question_raw: What name is given to that part of the enzyme where catalytic work is carried out?
questions.explanation: Enzymes are bio-catalyst that alter the rate of biochemical reactions in living organisms. The part of the enzyme where catalytic work is carried out is called the active site.

questions.question: The giant Redwood tree (Sequoia sempervirens) is a/an:
questions.question_raw: The giant Redwood tree (Sequoia sempervirens) is a/an:
questions.explanation: Gymnosperm. It is the sole living species of genus Sequoia. Its common name includes redwood, California redwood. It is an evergreen, long-living monoecious tree.

questions.question: Which plant has a fungal association in the form of mycorrhiza?
questions.question_raw: Which plant has a fungal association in the form of mycorrhiza?
questions.explanation: Roots in some genera of gymnosperms have a fungal association in the form of mycorrhizal (Pinus).

questions.question: Diatoms do not decay easily because:
questions.question_raw: Diatoms do not decay easily because:

options.text: They are abundant in saline water
Their body is impervious to water

questions.explanation: The walls of the diatoms are embedded with silica and thus the walls are indestructible.

questions.question: What makes up more than one-half the volume of neural tissue in our body?
questions.question_raw: What makes up more than one-half the volume of neural tissue in our body?
questions.explanation: The neuroglial cells which constitute the rest of the neural system protect and support neurons. Neuroglia makes up more than one-half the volume of neural tissue in our body.

questions.question: The nucleus was first described by:
questions.question_raw: The nucleus was first described by:
questions.explanation: Nucleus as a cell organelle was first described by Robert Brown as early as 1831.

questions.question: Monoterpene esters called pyrethroids found in flowers and leaves of chrysanthemum are used commercially for making ______.
questions.question_raw: Monoterpene esters called pyrethroids found in flowers and leaves of chrysanthemum are used commercially for making ______.
questions.explanation: Monoterpene eaters called pyrethroids found in flowers and leaves of chrysanthemum are used commercially for making insecticides.

questions.question: A group of closely related classes is called _______.
questions.question_raw: A group of closely related classes is called _______.
questions.explanation: Genus comprises a group of related species, Family has a group of related genera, the order has a group of related family. Hence, the correct option is Phylum.

questions.question: Blood consists of:
questions.question_raw: Blood consists of:
questions.explanation: Blood is a fluid connective tissue containing plasma. It contains red blood cells (RBC), white blood cells (WBC), and platelets suspended in the plasma.

questions.question: The algae that secrete and deposit calcium carbonate over their walls to contribute to producing coral reefs is______.
questions.question_raw: The algae that secrete and deposit calcium carbonate over their walls to contribute to producing coral reefs is______.
questions.explanation: Red algae in the order Corallinales, characterized by a thallus that is hard because of calcareous deposits which contribute to producing coral reefs in seawater.

questions.question: Which one of the following is a living fossil?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following is a living fossil?
questions.explanation: Limulus (the king crab or horse-shoe crab) continues to remain unchanged for the past 190 million years. It is, therefore, also called living fossil. Excretion occurs through four pairs of coxal glands. Respiration occurs through book gills.

questions.question: Organisms living in salty areas are called as
questions.question_raw: Organisms living in salty areas are called as
questions.explanation: Halos means salt and phile means loving.

questions.question: Which one of the following statements is true for cockroach?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following statements is true for cockroach?

options.text: The larval stage is called caterpillar
Anal styles are absent in females
The number of ovarioles in each ovary are ten

correct_option_texts: Anal styles are absent in females
questions.explanation: Male cockroaches bear a pair of short, thread-like anal styles in the 9th segment which is absent in females.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Enzyme becomes inactive below minimum temperature.
Reason (R): The inactivity of the enzymes is due to denaturation.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): Enzyme becomes inactive below minimum temperature.
Reason (R): The inactivity of the enzymes is due to denaturation.

options.text: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is true but R is false.
A is false but R is true.
Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.

correct_option_texts: A is true but R is false.
questions.explanation: Enzyme becomes inactive below minimum temperature. Low temperature preserves the enzymes in the inactive state. High temperature destroys enzymes by causing their denaturation.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Short term adaptations have little role in evolution.
Reason (R): They are acquired.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): Short term adaptations have little role in evolution.
Reason (R): They are acquired.

options.text: A is false but R is true.
Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is true but R is false.

correct_option_texts: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
questions.explanation: Short term adaptation (like defoliation of lives in autumn) are changes develop in living organisms in response to temporary environmental changes. These changes are temporary changes. Such changes are noninheritable i.e. acquired. As in these, DNA is not changed so these play no role in evolution. On the other hand long term adaptation (like type of beak in birds, modification of structures in plants) involve change in DNA hence they play an important role in evolution.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Rudolf Virchow modified the hypothesis of cell theory given by Schleiden and Schwann.
Reason (R): Cell theory says that all cells arise from pre-existing cells.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): Rudolf Virchow modified the hypothesis of cell theory given by Schleiden and Schwann.
Reason (R): Cell theory says that all cells arise from pre-existing cells.

options.text: A is false but R is true.
Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is true but R is false.

correct_option_texts: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
questions.explanation:
Schleiden and Schwann together formulated the cell theory. The theory, however, did not explain how new cells were formed. Rudolf Virchow (1855) first explained that cells divided and new cells are formed from pre-existing cells (Omins cellula e cellula). He modified the hypothesis of Schleiden and Schwann to give the cell theory a final shape. The present cell theory states that:
 all living organisms are composed of cells and products of cells.
 all cells arise from preexisting cells.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): The colony of Volvocales is called as coenobium.
Reason (R): Vegetative cells are arranged in a specific manner.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): The colony of Volvocales is called as coenobium.
Reason (R): Vegetative cells are arranged in a specific manner.

options.text: A is true but R is false.
A is false but R is true.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.

correct_option_texts: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
questions.explanation: Volvocales is the only order of the green algae in which the vegetative cell is actively motile. The colonies included in this order have a definite number of cells arranged in a specific manner. This type of colony is called a coenobium. The term coenobium is applied due to the protoplasmic connections hence the term coenocytic.

questions.question: Apart from excretion what is another major function of kidneys in mammals?
questions.question_raw: Apart from excretion what is another major function of kidneys in mammals?
questions.explanation: Osmoregulation is the process of maintaining the correct amount of water in body.

questions.question: Who divided animals into two groups based on the presence and absence of red blood cells?
questions.question_raw: Who divided animals into two groups based on the presence and absence of red blood cells?
questions.explanation: Aristotle used simple morphological characters to classify plants into trees, shrubs, and herbs. He also divided animals into two groups, those which had red blood and those that did not.

questions.question: Blood plasma is pale yellow in colour due to:
questions.question_raw: Blood plasma is pale yellow in colour due to:
questions.explanation: Blood plasma is pale yellow in colour due to the presence of bilirubin pigment in it. Blood appears red due to the presence of haemoglobin in RBC.

questions.question: No cell could live without ______.
questions.question_raw: No cell could live without ______.
questions.explanation: Enzymes are bio-catalyst. All the metabolic biochemical reactions are carried out with the help of the enzyme. No cell could survive without enzyme.

questions.question: A binomial nomenclature has ________ words.
questions.question_raw: A binomial nomenclature has ________ words.
questions.explanation: The first word in a biological name represents the genus while the second component denotes the specific epithet. Hence, the correct option is Two.

questions.question: In certain bacteria, the glycocalyx is formed into loose sheath layer is called as ________.
questions.question_raw: In certain bacteria, the glycocalyx is formed into loose sheath layer is called as ________.
questions.explanation: Glycocalyx differs in composition and thickness among different bacteria. It could be a loose sheath called the slime layer in some, while in others it may be thick and tough called the capsule. The cell wall determines the shape of the cell and provides strong structural support.

questions.question: Which of the following is responsible for peat formation ?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following is responsible for peat formation ?
questions.explanation: Sphagnum mosses not only dominate large areas of the boreal peatlands, but they have also formed these habitats by their growth and production of decay-resistant litter. As they invade, they can quickly transform a high-pH fen with little peat accumulation to a bog where large amounts of carbon are stored. They are also responsible for the many types of surface patterns observed in peatlands. Their ability to shape and transform mires can to a large extent be understood from the peculiar anatomy and biochemistry, a fact which makes the links between species’ traits and ecosystem functions in peatlands very strong.

questions.question: The body cavity is the cavity present between the body wall and the gut wall. In some animals, the body cavity is not lined by mesoderm. Such animals are called:
questions.question_raw: The body cavity is the cavity present between the body wall and the gut wall. In some animals, the body cavity is not lined by mesoderm. Such animals are called:
questions.explanation: Pseudocoelomates are those where the false body cavity is found between the body wall and gut wall.

questions.question: Which of the following organism is a good indicator of pollution in an environment?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following organism is a good indicator of pollution in an environment?
questions.explanation: Lichens can be used as air pollution indicators, especially of the concentration of sulfur dioxide in the atmosphere. Lichens are plants that grow in exposed places such as rocks or tree bark.

questions.question: Nissl granules in a nerve cell are now identified as_______.
questions.question_raw: Nissl granules in a nerve cell are now identified as_______.
questions.explanation: The Nissl granules in nerve cells are now identified as ribosomes after the discovery of an electron microscope that provides a highly magnified image for detailed study.

questions.question:
Different cells have different sizes. Arrange the following cells in ascending order of their size. Choose the correct option among the following.
 Mycoplasma
 Ostrich eggs
 Human RBC
 Bacteria
questions.question_raw:
Different cells have different sizes. Arrange the following cells in ascending order of their size. Choose the correct option among the following.

  3. Human RBC
  4. Bacteria

Egg of an ostrich is the largest cell and the smallest is mycoplasma having a size of 0.1 - 0.5 um.

questions.question: Which one of the following is fibrous protein?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following is fibrous protein?
questions.explanation: Collagen is a fibrous protein. It is the main structural protein in the extracellular space in the various connective tissues. It is the most abundant protein in mammals.

questions.question: In a hierarchical system of classification of plants, which of the following taxonomic rank ends in ceae?
questions.question_raw: In a hierarchical system of classification of plants, which of the following taxonomic rank ends in ceae?
questions.explanation: Plant families like Convolvulaceae, Solanaceae are included in the order Polymoniales mainly based on the floral characters. Hence, the correct option is Family.

questions.question: The fusion of protoplasm between to motile or non-motile gamete is called ________.
questions.question_raw: The fusion of protoplasm between to motile or non-motile gamete is called ________.
questions.explanation: Plasmogamy is the fusion of protoplasm of two cells which may be motile or non- motile. Plasmogamy occurs during sexual reproduction to form a diploid organism.

questions.question: The formation of erythrocytes in foetus takes place in:
questions.question_raw: The formation of erythrocytes in foetus takes place in:
questions.explanation: In the early fetus, erythropoiesis takes place in the mesodermal cells of the yolk sac. Later, the production of RBCs starts in the fetal liver and increases until the 2nd^ trimester. After this, the rate of synthesis begins to decline in the liver as erythropoiesis increases in the bone marrow. RBCs are still produced in the liver for approximately 1 week after birth.

questions.question: Which one is less general in characteristic as compared to the genus?
questions.question_raw: Which one is less general in characteristic as compared to the genus?
questions.explanation: Species is the lowest rank in the hierarchy. It means species contain the least general characters as compared to other categories.

questions.question: Cell drinking is another name of _______.
questions.question_raw: Cell drinking is another name of _______.
questions.explanation: Cell drinking is another name of pinocytosis. Pinocytosis, a process by which liquid droplets are ingested by living cells. Pinocytosis is one type of endocytosis, the general process by which cells engulf external substances, gathering them into special membrane-bound vesicles contained within the cell.

questions.question: Pteridophytes differ from mosses in having:
questions.question_raw: Pteridophytes differ from mosses in having:
questions.explanation: In bryophytes, the dominant phase in the life cycle is the gametophytic plant body. However, in pteridophytes, the main plant body is a sporophyte which is differentiated into true root, stem and leaves.

questions.question: The air bladder is present in_______.
questions.question_raw: The air bladder is present in_______.
questions.explanation: The air bladder is present in Actinopterygii. It is also known as ray-finned fish due to the presence of fin rays.

questions.question: Tendons connect:
questions.question_raw: Tendons connect:
questions.explanation: Tendons are dense connective tissue which provides strength and attach muscles to bones.

questions.question:

Various morphological features are used to describe a flowering plant. The plant is described beginning with its habit, vegetative characters – roots, stem and leaves and then floral characters inflorescence and flower parts. After describing various parts of plant, a floral diagram and a floral formula are presented. Fusion is indicated by enclosing the figure within bracket and adhesion by a line drawn above the symbols of the floral parts. A floral diagram provides information about the number of parts of a flower, their arrangement and the relation they have with one another. Some of the important families are Solanaceae is a large family, commonly called as the ‘potato family’ and Liliaceae Commonly called the ‘Lily family’.

 Floral formula also shows cohesion and adhesion within parts of whorls and between whorls.
 Fusion inform about the number of parts of a flower, their arrangement
 The position of the mother axis with respect to the flower is represented by a dot on the top of the floral diagram.
 In the floral formula, K stands for calyx corolla.
