questions.question: Venation is a term used to describe the pattern of arrangement of:
questions.question_raw: Venation is a term used to describe the pattern of arrangement of:

options.text: Veins and veinlets in a lamina

correct_option_texts: Veins and veinlets in a lamina
questions.explanation: Venation is the arrangement of veins and veinlets in leaf.

questions.question:
Which of the following structure of neural tissue acts like an insulating structure?
 Dendrite
 Axon
 Myelin
 Cyton
questions.question_raw:
Which of the following structure of neural tissue acts like an insulating structure?

  1. Dendrite
  2. Axon
  3. Myelin
  4. Cyton
questions.explanation: Myelin sheath consists of a fat-containing cell that insulates the axon. This insulation increases the rate of transmission.

questions.question: Lysosomes are rich in hydrolytic enzymes such as:
questions.question_raw: Lysosomes are rich in hydrolytic enzymes such as:
questions.explanation: Lysosomes are found to be very rich in almost all types of hydrolytic enzymes (hydrolases - lipases, proteases, carbohydrases) optimally active at the acidic pH. These enzymes are capable of digesting carbohydrates, proteins, lipids, and nucleic acids.

questions.question: Which of the following metabolites are incorrect pairs?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following metabolites are incorrect pairs?
questions.explanation: Ricin and abrin are toxins.

questions.question: Subunits of 80S ribosome are:
questions.question_raw: Subunits of 80S ribosome are:
questions.explanation:
Each ribosome consists of two subunits.
Eukaryotes have 80S ribosomes, each consisting of a small (40S) and large (60S) subunit.
Their 40S subunit has an 18S RNA (1900 nucleotides) and 33 proteins.
The large subunit is composed of a 5S RNA (120 nucleotides), 28S RNA (4700 nucleotides), a 5.8S RNA (160 nucleotides) subunits, and 46 proteins.

questions.question: Glycogen is a homopolymer made of :
questions.question_raw: Glycogen is a homopolymer made of :
questions.explanation: Glycogen is a multibranched polysaccharide of glucose that serves as a form of energy storage in animals, fungi, and bacteria. The polysaccharide structure represents the main storage form of glucose in the body.

mock_tests.instruction:
The Question Paper contains three sections.
 Section A has 24 questions. Attempt any 20 questions.
 Section B has 24 questions. Attempt any 20 questions.
 Section C has 12 questions. Attempt any 10 questions.
 All questions carry equal marks.
 There is no negative marking.

questions.question: The Darwin of the 20th century is:
questions.question_raw: The Darwin of the 20th century is:
questions.explanation: Born on 5 July 1904, in Kempten, Germany, ERNST MAYR, the Harvard University evolutionary biologist who has been called ‘The Darwin of the 20th century’, was one of the 100 greatest scientists of all time.

questions.question: Which of the following is not part of endomembrane system?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following is not part of endomembrane system?
questions.explanation: The endomembrane system includes endoplasmic reticulum (ER), Golgi complex, lysosomes, and vacuoles.

questions.question: Rhodophyta is commonly called red algae because of the predominance of pigment________.
questions.question_raw: Rhodophyta is commonly called red algae because of the predominance of pigment________.
questions.explanation: The algal class Rhodophyta are commonly called red algae because it contains a red pigment, r-phycoerythrin along with chlorophyll a and d.

questions.question: Pick the animals with true placenta__________.
questions.question_raw: Pick the animals with true placenta__________.
questions.explanation: The true placenta is present in Mongoose. Placenta help in the transfer of nutrients and respiratory gases from the mother to the growing foetus.

questions.question: Classification based on comparative cytological studies, the number of chromosome and behaviour of chromosome is called _______.
questions.question_raw: Classification based on comparative cytological studies, the number of chromosome and behaviour of chromosome is called _______.
questions.explanation: Cytotaxonomy is the branch of biology dealing with the relationships and classification of organisms using comparative studies of chromosomes.

questions.question: Where is ciliated epithelium found in the human body?
questions.question_raw: Where is ciliated epithelium found in the human body?
questions.explanation: The ciliated epithelium is mainly present in the inner surface of hollow organs like bronchioles and fallopian tubes.

questions.question: Who was the first to explain that cells divide?
questions.question_raw: Who was the first to explain that cells divide?
questions.explanation: The German doctor Rudolf Virchow proposed in 1855 that all cells result from the division of previously existing cells, and this idea became a key piece of modern cell theory.

questions.question: Many elements are found in living organisms either free or in the form of compounds. Which of the following is not found in living organisms?
questions.question_raw: Many elements are found in living organisms either free or in the form of compounds. Which of the following is not found in living organisms?
questions.explanation: Silicon is not found freely in nature, but it does occur in the bounded form as oxides and silicates, whereas magnesium, sodium, and iron are present in living organisms as ions.

questions.question: How much percent of vacuoles occupy the volume of the cell in plant cells?
questions.question_raw: How much percent of vacuoles occupy the volume of the cell in plant cells?
questions.explanation: In a mature cell, the vacuole may occupy as much as 90 percent of the cell volume, such that the rest of the cell contents are flattened against the cell membrane.

questions.question: The inhibition of enzyme activity by the presence of a chemical that competes with the substrate for binding to the active site of the enzyme is called:
questions.question_raw: The inhibition of enzyme activity by the presence of a chemical that competes with the substrate for binding to the active site of the enzyme is called:
questions.explanation: The inhibition of enzyme activity by the presence of a chemical that competes with the substrate for binding to the active site of the enzyme is called substrate analogue.

questions.question: Taxon is a:
questions.question_raw: Taxon is a:
questions.explanation: Each category in the taxonomic hierarchy represents the rank and is commonly termed as a taxon. Hence, the correct option is Ranking.

questions.question: How many essential amino acids are known?
questions.question_raw: How many essential amino acids are known?
questions.explanation: The 9 essential amino acids are histidine, isoleucine, leucine, lysine, methionine, phenylalanine, threonine, tryptophan, and valine.

questions.question: Algae with ribbon-shaped chloroplast _______.
questions.question_raw: Algae with ribbon-shaped chloroplast _______.
questions.explanation: Spirogyra is an alga that has a long thread-like structure having a ribbon-shaped chloroplast. Spirogyra is found in stagnant water forming a green layer around the surface called pond scum.

questions.question: Select one of the following pairs of important features distinguishing Gnetum from Cycas and Pinus and showing affinities with angiosperms.
questions.question_raw: Select one of the following pairs of important features distinguishing Gnetum from Cycas and Pinus and showing affinities with angiosperms.

options.text: Absence of rasin duct and leaf venation
Presence of vessels elements and the absence of archegonia

correct_option_texts: Presence of vessels elements and the absence of archegonia
questions.explanation: Gnetum belongs to order Gnetales. In gymnosperms except Gnetales xylem consists of xylem parenchyma and tracheids with bordered pits but lacks vessels. This shows that Gnetales are the most advanced among gymnosperms. They lack archegonia in female gametophytes thus showing similarity with angiosperms and acts as a connecting link between the gymnosperms and angiosperms.

questions.question: How bacteria reproduce during unfavourable conditions?
questions.question_raw: How bacteria reproduce during unfavourable conditions?
questions.explanation: Bacteria reproduce mainly by fission. Sometimes, under unfavourable conditions, they produce spores. They also reproduce by a sort of sexual reproduction by adopting a primitive type of DNA transfer from one bacterium to the other.

questions.question: In earthworm, two pairs of testes are present in:
questions.question_raw: In earthworm, two pairs of testes are present in:
questions.explanation: There are two pairs of testes present in the 10th and 11th segments. Their vasa deferentia run-up to the 18th segment where they join the prostatic duct.

questions.question: The cytoskeleton is made of:
questions.question_raw: The cytoskeleton is made of:
questions.explanation: An elaborate network of filamentous proteinaceous structures present in the cytoplasm is collectively referred to as the cytoskeleton.

questions.question: The primary structure of a protein molecule has:
questions.question_raw: The primary structure of a protein molecule has:
questions.explanation: Two ends - the carboxyl and amino-terminal.

questions.question: Which of the following can also be made in your home?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following can also be made in your home?
questions.explanation: In biology, an identification key is a printed or computer-aided device that aids the identification of biological entities, such as plants, animals, fossils, microorganisms, and pollen grains.

questions.question: The ciliated columnar epithelium cells in human are known to occur in:
questions.question_raw: The ciliated columnar epithelium cells in human are known to occur in:
questions.explanation: Ciliated columnar epithelium cells in humans occur in bronchioles and fallopian tube. They contain small hair-like cilia on the free surface. This helps to move egg into the fallopian tube. In bronchioles, it helps to move mucus or dust particles.

questions.question: In gymnosperms, cross-pollination is accompanied by-
questions.question_raw: In gymnosperms, cross-pollination is accompanied by-
questions.explanation: In Gymnosperms, cross-pollination is mainly accompanied by wind. The pollen grains are produced in large quantities and generally lighter.

questions.question: Lac is produced by lac insect as:
questions.question_raw: Lac is produced by lac insect as:

options.text: Excess food oozing out of the body

questions.explanation: Lac is obtained by lac insects as secretion from the body. This secretion is wounded around the cocoon of lac insects.

questions.question: In five kingdom of classification, Whittaker assigned eukaryotes to how many kingdoms?
questions.question_raw: In five kingdom of classification, Whittaker assigned eukaryotes to how many kingdoms?
questions.explanation: In Whittaker’s five kingdoms of classification, kingdom Protista, kingdom fungi, kingdom Plantae and kingdom Animalia are eukaryotic organisms. The kingdom monera includes prokaryotic organisms. Hence, four kingdoms out of five are eukayotes.

questions.question: The special venous connection between liver and intestine as well as the kidney and lower parts of the body present in frogs is called ________.
questions.question_raw: The special venous connection between liver and intestine as well as the kidney and lower parts of the body present in frogs is called ________.
questions.explanation: The renal portal system forms a special blood circulation system between liver and intestine. It also forms a special connection between the kidney and lower parts of the body present in frogs.

questions.question:

questions.question_raw:

options.text: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.

correct_option_texts: A is true but R is false.
questions.explanation: All the catalysts including those which hasten the metabolic conversion rate are proteins.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): In animals, carbohydrates are mostly translocated as sucrose.
Reason (R): In plants, excess of sugar is stored as starch.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): In animals, carbohydrates are mostly translocated as sucrose.
Reason (R): In plants, excess of sugar is stored as starch.

options.text: A is false but R is true.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is true but R is false.
Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.

correct_option_texts: A is false but R is true.
questions.explanation: In animals, carbohydrates are mostly translocated as glucose. Glucose is the simplest carbohydrate. Excess of glucose is polymerised and stored as starch in plants.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Prokaryotic cells have centrioles.
Reason (R): Centriole is not a membrane bound organelle.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): Prokaryotic cells have centrioles.
Reason (R): Centriole is not a membrane bound organelle.

options.text: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
A is false but R is true.
A is true but R is false.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.

correct_option_texts: A is false but R is true.
questions.explanation: Prokaryotes lack membrane-bound organelles but still, lack centriole because centriole is found only in animal cells.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Oltmansiella, Goniam and Pandorina are simple coenobial colonies.
Reason (R): The cells of colony lack protoplasmic connections.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): Oltmansiella, Goniam and Pandorina are simple coenobial colonies.
Reason (R): The cells of colony lack protoplasmic connections.

options.text: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
A is false but R is true.
A is true but R is false.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.

correct_option_texts: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
questions.explanation: The colonies of Volvocine line are called coenobium. The simple, primitive or less specialised coenobial forms of Oltmansiella, Gonium and Pandorina are only aggregations of similar Chlamydomonas - like cells with little differentiation and no division of labour. They consist of cells all of which are alike and able to reproduce. These cells are independent. Any cell separated from the colony is able to develop into a new colony by cell division. The cells lack any protoplasmic connections.

questions.question: How many chambers are there in the heart of a crocodile?
questions.question_raw: How many chambers are there in the heart of a crocodile?
questions.explanation: Crocodile belongs to reptile but have 4 chambered heart.

questions.question: When a freshwater protozoan is placed in marine water:
questions.question_raw: When a freshwater protozoan is placed in marine water:

options.text: The contractile vacuole increase in size

questions.explanation: When freshwater protozoan is placed in marine water, the contractile vacuole disappears because the function of the vacuole is to expel out excess water but in the case of marine water, the water should be retained instead of getting expelled. If the contractile vacuole will not disappear the cell will get dehydrated.

questions.question: Earthworms are known as Friends of farmers because:
questions.question_raw: Earthworms are known as Friends of farmers because:

options.text: They are used as meal for farmers

questions.explanation: Earthworms are known as ‘friends of farmers’ because they make burrows in the soil and make it porous which helps in respiration and penetration of the developing plant roots.

questions.question: Which of the following statements are not correct?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following statements are not correct?

options.text: The nucleic acid consists of ribose.
In DNA two strands are run in the opposite direction.
The purines and pyrimidines are paired with hydrogen bonds.
The backbone is formed by sugar-phosphate molecule.

correct_option_texts: The nucleic acid consists of ribose.
questions.explanation: A nucleic acid containing deoxyribose is called deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) while that which contains ribose is called ribonucleic acid (RNA).

questions.question: Rajaji national park is in ______.
questions.question_raw: Rajaji national park is in ______.
questions.explanation: Rajaji national park is an Indian national park and tiger reserve that encompasses the Shivaliks, near the foothill of the Himalayas in Uttrakhand.

questions.question: What is the basis of classification of chromosomes?
questions.question_raw: What is the basis of classification of chromosomes?
questions.explanation: The position of the centromere in the chromosome (which is constant to a given chromosome) varies i.e., it may occupy different positions. Based on this, four morphological shapes have been identified in chromosomes.

questions.question: Which classes of algae possess chlorophyll a, chlorophyll b, carotenoids and xanthophylls?
questions.question_raw: Which classes of algae possess chlorophyll a, chlorophyll b, carotenoids and xanthophylls?
questions.explanation: The class Chlorophyceae (Green Algae) contains chlorophyll a, chlorophyll b, carotenoids, and xanthophylls as their primary pigments. These pigments give them a distinct green color and help in photosynthesis.

questions.question: What is the main excretory waste in insects?
questions.question_raw: What is the main excretory waste in insects?
questions.explanation: The main nitrogenous excretory wastes of insects are uric acid. Uric acid is released in concentrated form. Other animals release nitrogenous wastes in form of ammonia and urea.

questions.question: Who is called as Father of taxonomy?
questions.question_raw: Who is called as Father of taxonomy?
questions.explanation: Carl Linnaeus, also known as Carl von Linné or Carolus Linnaeus, is often called the Father of Taxonomy. His system for naming, ranking, and classifying organisms is still in wide use today with many changes.

questions.question: In cockroach, a mushroom-shaped gland is present in:
questions.question_raw: In cockroach, a mushroom-shaped gland is present in:
questions.explanation: A characteristic mushroom-shaped gland is present in the 6th-7th abdominal segments of the cockroach. This gland functions as an accessory reproductive gland.

questions.question: Which of the following is part of the endomembrane system?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following is part of the endomembrane system?
questions.explanation: The endomembrane system includes endoplasmic reticulum (ER), Golgi complex, lysosomes, and vacuoles.

questions.question: Aminoacids have both an amino group and a carboxyl group in their structure. Which one of the following is an amino acid?
questions.question_raw: Aminoacids have both an amino group and a carboxyl group in their structure. Which one of the following is an amino acid?
questions.explanation:
Glycine is the simplest amino acid with hydrogen, as the R group.
Formic acid is the simplest carboxylic acid molecule. It is chemically HCOOH.
Glycerol is a polyol compound, which is chemically propane-1,2,3-triol and
Glycolic acid is a chemical, 2-hydroxyethanoic acid.

questions.question: The _________ is a fungal infection.
questions.question_raw: The _________ is a fungal infection.
questions.explanation: Ringworm, also known as dermatophytosis or tinea, is a fungal infection of the skin. The name “ringworm” is a misnomer since the infection is caused by a fungus, not a worm. Ringworm infection can affect both humans and animals.

questions.question: To which one of the following categories does adipose tissue belong?
questions.question_raw: To which one of the following categories does adipose tissue belong?
questions.explanation: Adipose tissue is a type of connective tissue consisting mainly of adipose cells or adipocytes, specialized to synthesize and contain large globules of fat.

questions.question: Which of the following is a defining characteristic of living organisms?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following is a defining characteristic of living organisms?
questions.explanation: Rest of the characters may not be apparent at every instance of time. But response to external stimuli can always be confirmed as and when required. So, this is considered as a defining characteristic of living organisms.

questions.question: The membrane complex formed in the folding of the plasma membrane in prokaryotic cells is called ________.
questions.question_raw: The membrane complex formed in the folding of the plasma membrane in prokaryotic cells is called ________.
questions.explanation: A special membranous structure is a mesosome which is formed by the extensions of the plasma membrane into the cell.

questions.question: The phloem of angiosperms differ from that of other vascular plants by the presence of:
questions.question_raw: The phloem of angiosperms differ from that of other vascular plants by the presence of:
questions.explanation: Gymnosperms and lower vascular plants have sieve cells as their sieve elements, while sieve tubes and companion cells are characteristic of flowering plants (angiosperms). Phloem transports food from leaves to all other parts of plants.

questions.question: In Molluscs, the rasping feeding organ is:
questions.question_raw: In Molluscs, the rasping feeding organ is:
questions.explanation: The mouth of molluscs contains a file-like rasping organ for feeding, called a radula.

questions.question: Which one of the following is not a connective tissue?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following is not a connective tissue?
questions.explanation:
Blood, bone, lymph forms the connective tissue that connects the different parts of the body
Nerve cells are parts of neural system or nervous system that transmit information from one part of the body to others.

Instructions for Question 49 to 54:
The androecium is composed of stamens. Each stamen that represents the male reproductive organ consists of a stalk or a filament and an anther. Each anther is usually bilobed and each lobe has two chambers, the pollen-sacs. Stamens of flowers may be united with other members such as petals or among themselves. The stamens may be epipetalous or epiphyllous. A flower is a modified shoot wherein the shoot apical meristem changes to floral meristem. Internodes do not elongate and the axis gets condensed. The apex produces different kinds of floral appendages laterally at successive nodes instead of leaves. The arrangement of flowers on the floral axis is termed an inflorescence.

The stamens may be epipetalous or epiphyllous. A flower is a modified shoot wherein the shoot apical meristem changes to floral meristem. Internodes do not elongate and the axis gets condensed. The apex produces different kinds of floral appendages laterally at successive nodes instead of leaves. The arrangement of flowers on the floral axis is termed an inflorescence.
Which of the following shows variation in the length of filaments within a flower?

questions.question:
Instructions for Question 49 to 54:
The flower is the reproductive unit in the angiosperms. It is meant for sexual reproduction. A typical flower has four different kinds of whorls arranged successively on the swollen end of the stalk or pedicel, called thalamus or receptacle. These are calyx, corolla, androecium and gynoecium. Calyx and corolla are accessory organs, while androecium and gynoecium are reproductive organs. In symmetry, the flower may be actinomorphic (radial symmetry) or zygomorphic (bilateral symmetry). Based on the position of calyx, corolla and androecium in respect of the ovary on the thalamus, the flowers are described as hypogynous, perigynous and epigynous. A flower may be trimerous, tetramerous or pentamerous when the floral appendages are in multiple of 3, 4 or 5, respectively.
In some flowers like ________ the colyx and corolla are not distinct and term as perianth.

questions.question: Who first saw and described a live cell?
questions.question_raw: Who first saw and described a live cell?
questions.explanation: The first man to witness a live cell under a microscope was Anton van Leeuwenhoek, who in 1674 described the algae Spirogyra. Van Leeuwenhoek probably also saw bacteria.

questions.question: Primary metabolites are always found in animal cells. Which of the following is not an example of a primary metabolite?
questions.question_raw: Primary metabolites are always found in animal cells. Which of the following is not an example of a primary metabolite?
questions.explanation: Primary metabolites include energy-rich fuel molecules, such as sucrose and starch, structural components such as cellulose, informational molecules such as DNA (deoxyribonucleic acid) and RNA (ribonucleic acid), and pigments, such as chlorophyll.

questions.question: Middle lamella is composed of:
questions.question_raw: Middle lamella is composed of:
questions.explanation: The middle lamella is a layer mainly of calcium pectate which holds or glues the different neighbouring cells together.

questions.question: Which helps in transporting glucose into cells?
questions.question_raw: Which helps in transporting glucose into cells?
questions.explanation: Glut-4 enables glucose transport into cells.
